what: Important : à propos d’un dossier .git déjà présent
title: Mise en ligne sur GitHub (FunTraits/intraitR)
text: J’ai tenté d’initialiser git directement dans ce dossier depuis mon environnement, mais celui-ci ne peut pas supprimer certains fichiers temporaires que git crée puis nettoie normalement (limitation de mon bac à sable sur ce dossier synchronisé OneDrive, pas de votre machine). Résultat : il y peut-être un dossier .git incomplet et verrouillé dans intraitR/. Supprimez-le d’abord depuis votre propre machine (Finder/Explorateur, ou un terminal local) avant de continuer : Cette suppression devrait fonctionner normalement chez vous (la restriction ne s’applique qu’à mon environnement). Tout le reste du contenu du dossier (code R, tests, DESCRIPTION, etc.) est intact et n’pas été affecté.
code: cd /chemin/vers/Intrait_Package/intraitR rm -rf .git

what: 1. Initialiser git et faire le premier commit (sur votre machine)
title: Mise en ligne sur GitHub (FunTraits/intraitR)
text: Dans un terminal, à la racine du dossier intraitR :
code: cd /chemin/vers/Intrait_Package/intraitR git init git add -A git commit -m "intraitR v1.0.0"

what: 2. Créer le dépôt vide sur GitHub
title: Mise en ligne sur GitHub (FunTraits/intraitR)
text: Sur https://github.com/organizations/FunTraits/repositories/new (ou gh repo create FunTraits/intraitR --public --source=. --remote=origin si vous avez la CLI gh installée et authentifiée) : Nom du dépôt : intraitR Ne cochez aucune case d’initialisation (pas de README, pas de .gitignore, pas de licence) : votre dépôt local en contient déjà, et GitHub refusera sinon la première synchronisation (historiques divergents). Visibilité : public ou privé, selon votre préférence.

what: 3. Relier le dépôt local et pousser
title: Mise en ligne sur GitHub (FunTraits/intraitR)
text: (Remplacez l’URL par git@github.com:FunTraits/intraitR.git si vous utilisez l’authentification SSH plutôt que HTTPS.)
code: git remote add origin https://github.com/FunTraits/intraitR.git git branch -M main git push -u origin main

what: Ce qui a été préparé pour vous
title: Mise en ligne sur GitHub (FunTraits/intraitR)
text: DESCRIPTION, README.md, inst/CITATION : toutes les références à l’ancienne URL provisoire (aureletoussaint/intraitR) ont été remplacées par FunTraits/intraitR. .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml : intégration continue standard (usethis::use_github_action("check-standard")), qui exécutera R CMD check sur Linux (R release, devel, oldrel), macOS et Windows à chaque push/pull request. .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml : construit et déploie automatiquement un site de documentation sur gh-pages à chaque push sur la branche par défaut (voir _pkgdown.yml pour la configuration de l’index de référence) ; il sera consultable à https://funtraits.github.io/intraitR/ après le premier déploiement réussi. .github/workflows/test-coverage.yaml : calcule la couverture de tests (covr::package_coverage()) et l’envoie à Codecov à chaque push. Nécessite un secret de dépôt CODECOV_TOKEN (à créer sur https://codecov.io après avoir lié le dépôt) pour que l’envoi fonctionne ; sans ce secret, le workflow s’exécute mais l’étape d’envoi échoue silencieusement en pull request externe. Badges : les quatre badges de statut (R-CMD-check, couverture de tests, Codecov, pkgdown) sont déjà dans README.md ; ils s’activeront automatiquement une fois les workflows correspondants exécutés au moins une fois sur GitHub. .gitignore : complété pour exclure .DS_Store, ainsi que deux fichiers de test locaux qui traînaient dans le dossier (specimens.tps et P5180033.jpg, produits par votre essai de digitize_landmarks()) — ils ne seront donc pas poussés sur GitHub une fois que vous aurez lancé git add. Si vous souhaitez au contraire verser une vraie photo d’exemple ou des données digitalisées réelles au dépôt, retirez les lignes correspondantes du .gitignore (idéalement sous un chemin dédié comme data-raw/ plutôt qu’à la racine).

title: Mise en ligne sur GitHub (FunTraits/intraitR)
text: BugReports: (dans DESCRIPTION) pointe déjà vers github.com/FunTraits/intraitR/issues. Le site pkgdown (voir ci-dessus) se déploiera automatiquement au premier push sur main ; activez GitHub Pages sur la branche gh-pages dans Settings > Pages si ce n’est pas fait automatiquement par le workflow. Pour une soumission CRAN, cran-comments.md et SUBMISSION_NOTES.md restent à jour comme pense-bête. devtools::test() été exécuté avec succès sur votre poste pour la v1.0.0 (465 tests passés, 0 échec après correction d’un bug de précédence d’opérateur dans un test de régression — voir NEWS.md). La v1.1.0 (ajout de method multi-mesures et de compare_functional_richness()) n’pas encore eu son propre run réel ; relancez devtools::test() puis devtools::check(cran = TRUE) avant toute soumission effective.

what: Notes de finalisation avant soumission CRAN
title: Notes de finalisation avant soumission CRAN
text: Ce package été écrit intégralement à la main (code R + DESCRIPTION + NAMESPACE + Rd via roxygen2 non exécuté), car l’environnement dans lequel il été généré ne dispose ni de R, ni d’un accès root, ni d’un accès réseau vers CRAN — il n’donc pas été possible d’exécuter devtools::document(), devtools::check() ou R CMD build/check pour valider automatiquement le paquet. Une relecture statique (équilibrage des parenthèses/accolades sur l’ensemble des fichiers R, cohérence entre les tags @export/@importFrom et le fichier NAMESPACE) été effectuée, et une incohérence trouvée lors de cette relecture (print.intrait_morphospace manquant du NAMESPACE) été corrigée. Mais une vérification complète sous R reste nécessaire avant toute soumission.

what: Mise à jour v1.1.0 : method multi-mesures + compare_functional_richness()
title: Notes de finalisation avant soumission CRAN
text: Ajouté après le premier run réel de v1.0.0, et donc de nouveau non exécuté sous R dans cet environnement (toujours pas de R disponible ici) : bootstrap_functional_space() et species_sensitivity() acceptent désormais un argument method ("convexhull", comportement par défaut inchangé ; "dendrogram", "tpd", "hypervolume"), et une nouvelle fonction compare_functional_richness() exécute plusieurs méthodes sur les mêmes données et tabule les résultats. Point d’attention prioritaire avant soumission : les appels à TPD::TPDsMean()/TPD::TPDc()/TPD::REND() et hypervolume::hypervolume_gaussian()/estimate_bandwidth()/get_volume() ont été écrits à partir de la documentation CRAN de référence de ces deux packages (manuels .Rd lus au moment de l’écriture, via recherche web puisqu’aucun R local n’était disponible pour vérifier interactivement ?TPD::TPDsMean etc.), et non testés par exécution réelle. Avant toute soumission : Installer TPD et hypervolume sur votre poste et lancer les tests method = "tpd"/method = "hypervolume" de test-bootstrap_functional_space.R, test-species_sensitivity.R, et test-compare_functional_richness.R (protégés par testthat::skip_if_not_installed(), donc silencieusement ignorés sans ces packages — vérifiez qu’ils s’exécutent bien et passent, pas seulement qu’ils sont “skip”). Vérifier en particulier que trait_ranges (grille fixe passée à TPD::TPDsMean()) et kde.bandwidth (largeur de bande fixe passée à hypervolume::hypervolume_gaussian()) acceptent bien le format construit par .fspace_richness_setup() dans la version de ces packages installée sur votre poste (packageVersion("TPD"), packageVersion("hypervolume")). method = "dendrogram" ne dépend que de stats::hclust() (aucune nouvelle dépendance) et été raisonné à la main (longueur totale de branches UPGMA, Petchey & Gaston 2002) ; à vérifier aussi par un vrai devtools::test(), par prudence, comme pour tout le reste du paquet. DESCRIPTION : TPD et hypervolume ont été ajoutés à Suggests ; Version: est passé à 1.1.0 et un champ Date: été ajouté.

what: Mise à jour v1.0.0 : premier devtools::test() réel
title: Notes de finalisation avant soumission CRAN
text: Pour la version 1.0.0, devtools::test() enfin été exécuté sur un poste avec R (celui du mainteneur), et non plus seulement relu statiquement comme pour toutes les versions précédentes. Résultat : 465 tests passés, 0 échec, 5 avertissements attendus (documentés ci-dessous) et 6 tests ignorés (skip) pour des raisons attendues (chemins nécessitant un package absent volontairement du test, ou une session R interactive). Un seul problème été détecté : test-trait_disparity.R, test de régression sur iter à 2 groupes : %% en réalité une précédence plus forte que / en R (voir ?Syntax), si bien que x %% ((0:5) + 1) / 6 s’analysait en (x %% ((0:5) + 1)) / 6 au lieu de x %% (((0:5) + 1) / 6) — c’est-à-dire une erreur dans le test lui-même, pas dans trait_disparity() (dont les autres tests, y compris un test de puissance statistique réelle, passaient déjà). Corrigé en parenthésant explicitement le dénominateur ; voir NEWS.md. Ceci confirme, posteriori, la fiabilité de la méthode suivie jusqu’ici en l’absence de R (relecture manuelle systématique + réimplémentation Python indépendante de la logique statistique) : aucun bug de logique métier n’été détecté dans le code du paquet lui-même par ce premier run réel, seulement un bug de syntaxe R localisé dans un test. Il reste néanmoins recommandé de lancer devtools::check(cran = TRUE) (pas seulement devtools::test()) avant toute soumission, celui-ci vérifiant des aspects que test() ne couvre pas (documentation, exemples, NAMESPACE, taille du paquet, etc.).

previous_headings: Mise à jour v1.0.0 : premier devtools::test() réel
what: Ajout v0.2.0 : protocole FISHMORPH (Brosse et al., 2021)
title: Notes de finalisation avant soumission CRAN
text: fishmorph_segments(), fishmorph_ratios(), trait_space(), plot_fishmorph_points() et simulate_fishmorph_points() implémentent le schéma de digitalisation à 21/22 points et les 9 ratios de Brosse et al. (2021), tel que défini dans votre figure “Points Orga”. Points à vérifier en priorité sur votre poste : - Les indices de landmarks (1-22) et leurs correspondances aux mesures (Bl, Bd, Hd, Eh, Mo, PFi, PFl, Ed, Jl, CPd, CFd) ont été retranscrits à la main depuis votre figure ; reproduisez le calcul sur un spécimen réel digitalisé pour confirmer l’alignement exact des points, en particulier pour Eh (7-8) et le point de courbure optionnel (22), moins explicites sur la figure que les autres mesures. - Les tests unitaires de fishmorph_segments() utilisent une configuration de points construite à la main (distances connues) et vérifient les 11 segments et la mise à l’échelle ; ceci couvre la logique de calcul mais ne remplace pas une validation sur vos propres photos digitalisées. - trait_space() n’utilise que des fonctions de base R (stats::prcomp, stats::cmdscale, stats::dist), sans nouvelle dépendance.

what: Étapes à effectuer sur votre poste (R + RStudio déjà installés)
title: Notes de finalisation avant soumission CRAN
text: Installer les dépendances si nécessaire : Régénérer NAMESPACE et man/*.Rd à partir des commentaires roxygen2 (déjà présents dans chaque fichier R/*.R) : Cela doit reproduire un NAMESPACE identique à celui fourni ; sinon, devtools::document() fait foi. Lancer les tests unitaires : Construire la vignette et vérifier le paquet dans son ensemble : Corriger toute erreur, avertissement (WARNING) ou note (NOTE) avant soumission. Points à surveiller en particulier : Les noms d’arguments exacts des fonctions geomorph utilisées (gpagen(), gm.prcomp(), geomorph.data.frame(), morphol.disparity(), procD.lm(), two.d.array(), arrayspecs()) ont été écrits de mémoire d’après une version récente de geomorph ; vérifiez qu’ils correspondent bien à la version de geomorph installée sur votre poste (packageVersion("geomorph")), en particulier la structure retournée par gm.prcomp() (élément $rotation). DESCRIPTION : URL:/BugReports: pointent vers https://github.com/FunTraits/intraitR (dépôt créé le 2026-07-01 ; voir GITHUB_SETUP.md pour la mise en ligne). Si le dépôt venait à changer de nom ou d’organisation, pensez à mettre à jour ces deux champs (ainsi que inst/CITATION et l’exemple remotes::install_github() du README.md). Vérifiez le champ Version: (actuellement 1.1.0 ; voir NEWS.md pour l’historique complet des versions) et le champ Date: (actuellement 2026-07-04). (Optionnel) Générer un jeu de données statique inclus dans le paquet : exécutez data-raw/simulate_data.R (voir les instructions dans ce fichier et dans R/data.R) si vous préférez livrer fish_landmarks comme objet de données plutôt que de le générer à la volée avec simulate_fish_landmarks(). Vérifier win-builder / R-hub avant soumission : Compléter cran-comments.md avec les résultats réels de devtools::check() puis soumettre via :
code: install.packages(c("geomorph", "devtools", "roxygen2", "testthat", "knitr", "rmarkdown")) devtools::document() devtools::test() devtools::check(cran = TRUE) devtools::check_win_devel() rhub::check_for_cran()  # ou usethis::use_github_action("check-standard") devtools::submit_cran()

title: Notes de finalisation avant soumission CRAN
text: Le paquet ne dépend que de fonctions geomorph appelées explicitement via geomorph::fonction() (aucun library(geomorph) requis), ce qui est conforme aux bonnes pratiques CRAN pour les paquets en Imports. Aucune donnée binaire (data/*.rda) n’est livrée par défaut : tous les exemples, tests et la vignette utilisent simulate_fish_landmarks(), ce qui évite toute dépendance à un fichier de données pré-calculé et garantit une reproductibilité totale via seed. read_tps() réimplémente un analyseur de fichiers .tps en base R (plutôt que de s’appuyer sur geomorph::readland.tps()) afin d’exposer explicitement le facteur d’échelle (SCALE=) nécessaire à linear_distances()/morpho_ratios() ; le cœur statistique (GPA, PCA de forme, ANOVA procustéenne, disparité) reste entièrement délégué à geomorph, conformément à votre préférence.

title: Introduction to intraitR
text: intraitR supports complete workflow analysis morphological traits freshwater fish, digitized landmark coordinates morphological ratios, morphological space, intraspecific variability, measurement error. vignette walks workflow end end using simulated data set, runs without real specimen data. statistical core package (Generalised Procrustes Analysis, shape principal component analysis, Procrustes ANOVA morphological disparity) delegated geomorph package. intraitR adds fish-specific conveniences top: file import, linear ratios, reporting functions geared towards ecomorphological questions.

title: Introduction to intraitR
text: Landmark data can imported tpsDig-formatted .tps file [read_tps()], generic long-format CSV/data.frame [read_landmarks_csv()]. vignette instead simulate data set three artificial species, digitized three times per individual, using [simulate_fish_landmarks()]: Every specimen fish$metadata tagged species, population, simulated standard length, digitization replicate number. single configuration can inspected visually [plot_landmarks()]:  original specimen photographs available, plot_landmarks() ([plot_fishmorph_points()], ) can overlay landmarks directly photograph via background_image, useful visual check landmark placed body outline: landmarks yet digitized, [digitize_landmarks()] provides interactive, point--click front end (built [geomorph::digitize2d()]) digitizes directly specimen photographs returns ready--use "intrait_landmarks" object; requires live interactive graphics session run inside vignette, intended usage :
code: set.seed(1) fish <- simulate_fish_landmarks(   n_per_species = 15,   species = c("Species_A", "Species_B", "Species_C"),   n_landmarks = 12,   n_replicates = 3 ) fish #> <intrait_landmarks> #>   135 specimens, 12 landmarks, 2 dimensions #>   Scale available for 135/135 specimens #>   Metadata columns: specimen, species, population, standard_length_mm, replicate head(fish$metadata) #>                                  specimen   species population #> Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep3 Species_A_ind01_rep3 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A      Pop_2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A      Pop_2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep3 Species_A_ind02_rep3 Species_A      Pop_2 #>                      standard_length_mm replicate #> Species_A_ind01_rep1               74.4         1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2               74.4         2 #> Species_A_ind01_rep3               74.4         3 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1               95.3         1 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2               95.3         2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep3               95.3         3 plot_landmarks(fish, specimen = 1) plot_landmarks(fish, specimen = 1, background_image = "specimen1.jpg") lm <- digitize_landmarks(   images = c("specimen1.jpg", "specimen2.jpg", "specimen3.jpg"),   scheme = "fishmorph", tpsfile = "specimens.tps" )

title: Introduction to intraitR
text: [gpa_fish()] superimposes configurations, removing differences position, orientation scale: gpa$Csize holds centroid size, standard size measure retained alongside Procrustes shape coordinates.
code: gpa <- gpa_fish(fish) gpa #> <intrait_gpa> Procrustes-aligned landmark configurations #>   135 specimens, 12 landmarks, 2 dimensions #>   Converged in 2 iteration(s) #>   Centroid size: mean = 118.551, range = [81.630, 145.831] #>   No Procrustes-distance outliers flagged (see $outlier_screen)

title: Introduction to intraitR
text: Classical fish morphometric traits expressed ratios linear inter-landmark distances reference distance (typically standard length). Distance pairs defined landmark index computed raw (pre-Procrustes) coordinates, since Procrustes superimposition removes size:
code: distances <- list(SL = c(1, 7), BD = c(3, 10), HL = c(1, 4)) morpho_ratios(fish, distances, norm_by = "SL")[1:5, ] #>                                  specimen   species population #> Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep3 Species_A_ind01_rep3 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A      Pop_2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A      Pop_2 #>                      standard_length_mm replicate BD_ratio HL_ratio #> Species_A_ind01_rep1               74.4         1   0.3938   0.5144 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2               74.4         2   0.3935   0.5166 #> Species_A_ind01_rep3               74.4         3   0.3943   0.5132 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1               95.3         1   0.3829   0.5181 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2               95.3         2   0.3926   0.5158

title: Introduction to intraitR
text: morphospace built Principal Component Analysis Procrustes shape coordinates. default, plot() displays group individual points, “spider” dashed segments linking every point group mean, group mean , 95% dispersion ellipse (style = "spider"); pass style = "hull" classical convex-hull display, style = "density" non-parametric kernel-density contour (useful group’s points visibly skewed multimodal, bivariate-normal ellipse poor fit), style = "none" plain points. group legend drawn just outside plot box default (legend_position = "outside"), never overlaps data; pass standard legend() keyword (e.g. "topright") draw inside plot box instead, previous versions. Axis limits computed fully contain ellipse/hull/contour, just raw points, none ever clipped plot box edge. groups represents species identity, legend_title, legend_italic, abbreviate_species produce properly typeset “Species” legend (e.g. "Barbatula barbatula" displayed italic B. barbatula):
code: ms <- morpho_space(gpa, groups = fish$metadata$species) ms #> <intrait_morphospace> #>   Axes PC1/PC2, variance explained: 37.1% / 7.7% #>   135 specimens, 3 groups plot(ms, legend_title = "Species", legend_italic = TRUE, abbreviate_species = TRUE) plot(ms, style = "hull", legend_title = "Species", legend_italic = TRUE,      abbreviate_species = TRUE) plot(ms, style = "density", legend_title = "Species", legend_italic = TRUE,      abbreviate_species = TRUE)

title: Introduction to intraitR
text: [intraspecific_variability()] reports shape-based disparity measure (Procrustes variance per group, permutation test) classical coefficients variation chosen linear traits: [itv_index()] complements formal variance-partitioning approach (Violle et al., 2012; de Bello et al., 2011): percentage trait’s total variance lies within species (intraspecific trait variability, ITV) versus species (interspecific):  fish$metadata also records population nested within species, ITV component can split -population within-population (residual) part, following within-/among-population distinction commonly used ITV meta-analyses (e.g. Siefert et al., 2015):
code: ratios <- morpho_ratios(fish, distances, norm_by = "SL") iv <- intraspecific_variability(   gpa = gpa,   groups = fish$metadata$species,   traits = ratios[, c("BD_ratio", "HL_ratio")],   iter = 199 ) iv #> <intrait_variability> #> -- Shape (Procrustes variance) disparity -- #>  #> Call: #> geomorph::morphol.disparity(f1 = coords ~ 1, groups = ~groups,   #>     iter = iter, data = gdf, print.progress = FALSE)  #>  #>  #>  #> Randomized Residual Permutation Procedure Used #> 200 Permutations #>  #> Procrustes variances for defined groups #>   Species_A   Species_B   Species_C  #> 0.002775240 0.001398002 0.002362385  #>  #>  #> Pairwise absolute differences between variances #>              Species_A    Species_B    Species_C #> Species_A 0.0000000000 0.0013772378 0.0004128544 #> Species_B 0.0013772378 0.0000000000 0.0009643834 #> Species_C 0.0004128544 0.0009643834 0.0000000000 #>  #>  #> P-Values #>           Species_A Species_B Species_C #> Species_A     1.000     0.005     0.035 #> Species_B     0.005     1.000     0.005 #> Species_C     0.035     0.005     1.000 #>  #>  #> -- Coefficient of variation (%) of linear traits -- #>      group    trait  n      mean         sd cv_percent #>  Species_A BD_ratio 45 0.3828511 0.02152809   5.623096 #>  Species_B BD_ratio 45 0.3580489 0.01574101   4.396330 #>  Species_C BD_ratio 45 0.3381333 0.01598622   4.727785 #>  Species_A HL_ratio 45 0.5239222 0.01210852   2.311130 #>  Species_B HL_ratio 45 0.5190244 0.01362550   2.625213 #>  Species_C HL_ratio 45 0.5137244 0.01029254   2.003514 itv <- itv_index(ratios[, c("BD_ratio", "HL_ratio")], groups = fish$metadata$species) itv #> <intrait_itv> (species-level)  #>   2 trait(s), 3 groups #>  #> -- Per trait -- #>     trait ss_total ss_between ss_within pct_interspecific pct_itv #>  BD_ratio   0.0877     0.0452    0.0425           51.5009 48.4991 #>  HL_ratio   0.0216     0.0023    0.0193           10.8273 89.1727 #>  #> -- Multivariate summary (standardised traits) -- #>  ss_total ss_between ss_within pct_interspecific pct_itv #>       268    83.5197  184.4803           31.1641 68.8359 plot(itv) itv_nested <- itv_index(   ratios[, c("BD_ratio", "HL_ratio")],   groups = fish$metadata$species,   nested = fish$metadata$population ) itv_nested #> <intrait_itv> (nested: species / population)  #>   2 trait(s), 3 groups, 6 nested levels #>  #> -- Per trait -- #>     trait ss_total ss_between ss_population ss_residual ss_within #>  BD_ratio   0.0877     0.0452        0.0065      0.0360    0.0425 #>  HL_ratio   0.0216     0.0023        0.0005      0.0188    0.0193 #>  pct_interspecific pct_itv pct_itv_between_pop pct_itv_within_pop #>            51.5009 48.4991              7.4186            41.0806 #>            10.8273 89.1727              2.1858            86.9870 #>  #> -- Multivariate summary (standardised traits) -- #>  ss_total ss_between ss_population ss_residual ss_within pct_interspecific #>       268 title: Introduction to intraitR
text: Since simulated data set includes three digitization replicates per individual, [measurement_error()] can quantify much observed variance attributable digitization noise rather genuine biological variation, following Procrustes ANOVA (Fruciano, 2016): single univariate trait (e.g. body depth ratio measured individuals multiple times), classical ANOVA-based percent measurement error repeatability (Bailey & Byrnes, 1990) also available:
code: individual_id <- sub("_rep[0-9]+$", "", fish$metadata$specimen)  me <- measurement_error(gpa, individual = individual_id, method = "procrustes", iter = 199) me #> <intrait_measurement_error> #>  Method: Procrustes ANOVA measurement error (Fruciano, 2016)  #>  #>             Df       SS        MS     Rsq     F      Z Pr(>F)    #> individual  44 0.281112 0.0063889 0.95583 44.26 10.003  0.005 ** #> Residuals   90 0.012991 0.0001443 0.04417                        #> Total      134 0.294103                                          #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 me_ratio <- measurement_error(   data.frame(value = ratios$BD_ratio, individual = individual_id),   individual = "individual",   method = "anova" ) me_ratio #> <intrait_measurement_error> #>  Method: ANOVA-based measurement error (Bailey & Byrnes, 1990)  #>  #>             Df   Sum Sq   Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)     #> individual  44 0.085200 0.0019364  69.403 < 2.2e-16 *** #> Residuals   90 0.002511 0.0000279                       #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #>  Percent measurement error (%ME): 1.42% #>  Repeatability (R): 0.958

title: Introduction to intraitR

title: Introduction to intraitR
text: intraitR also implements specific digitization trait protocol used build FISHMORPH database (Brosse et al., 2021), based fixed scheme 21 landmarks per specimen (snout, caudal fin basis, body depth, head depth, eye position diameter, mouth, pectoral fin, caudal peduncle depth, caudal fin depth) plus embedded scale bar, optionally 22nd point correct standard length body curvature picture. simulate small data set following exact scheme [simulate_fishmorph_points()], inspect digitization one specimen [plot_fishmorph_points()]:  [fishmorph_segments()] computes 11 linear measurements protocol directly digitized points, automatically converting pixel units centimetres using embedded scale bar (landmarks 20-21): [fishmorph_ratios()] derives 9 unitless FISHMORPH ratios measurements. no_caudal_fin, ventral_mouth, no_pectoral_fin arguments implement special-case rules Villéger et al. (2010) species unusual morphologies (e.g. visible caudal fin, ventrally positioned mouth, pectoral fins): Finally, [trait_space()] builds functional trait space numeric trait table — 9 FISHMORPH ratios — Principal Component Analysis (Principal Coordinate Analysis, method = "pcoa"). default, traits log10(x + 1)-transformed (+ 1 accommodating ratios can legitimately equal 0 Villéger et al., 2010, exception rules) centred scaled unit variance ordination (log_transform = TRUE, scale = TRUE); set either FALSE skip step. morpho_space(), plot() defaults spider/ellipse display per group:  Missing trait values, common real ecomorphological data sets, handled via na_action argument: "fail" (default) errors, "omit" drops incomplete rows, "impute_mean"/"impute_group_mean" fill missing values column within-group means, "missforest" uses nonparametric random-forest imputation (missForest::missForest(), Stekhoven & Bühlmann, 2012), exploiting correlations among traits (groups, auxiliary predictor) rather filling every missing value trait mean: option reports via message() many rows values affected (, "missforest", --bag imputation error). [trait_disparity()] complements trait_space() permutation test whether groups differ multivariate trait dispersion (trace group’s trait covariance matrix), computed full standardised trait matrix rather two axes shown plot : [bootstrap_functional_space()] asks related different question: representing species real individuals, rather centroid, inflate estimated functional space (n-dimensional convex-hull volume PCA space)? n_boot bootstrap “communities” draws one individual random per species computes resulting convex-hull volume, compares distribution single centroid-based reference volume, following Bertrand (2026); requires geometry package (Suggested, installed default):  [species_sensitivity()] asks species drive difference: species’ centroid replaced, one individual time, individual’s position (every species fixed centroid), resulting percent change functional richness summarised per species mean effect min-max range (Bertrand, 2026):  functions accept method argument beyond default "convexhull": "dendrogram" (UPGMA functional dendrogram branch length, Petchey & Gaston 2002, extra package required), "tpd" (Trait Probability Density, Carmona et al. 2019), "hypervolume" (Gaussian-kernel hypervolume, Blonder et al. 2014, 2018). Since four measures scale, [compare_functional_richness()] runs several data tabulates percent change bootstrap p-value side side, agreement (disagreement) across methods can assessed:  fishmorph_segments() fishmorph_ratios() return plain data frames, compose directly [summary_traits()] [intraspecific_variability()] (traits-mode) exactly ratios [morpho_ratios()] earlier vignette.

title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish

code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("FunTraits/intraitR") install.packages("geomorph")

title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: Import landmark coordinates: read_tps() (tpsDig files), read_landmarks_csv() (generic long-format CSV/data.frame), read_landmarks_xlsx() (generic wide-format Excel sheet, one row per specimen one X/Y column pair per landmark – layout produced directly manual digitization spreadsheets); generate simulated example data set simulate_fish_landmarks(). Alternatively, digitize_landmarks() digitizes landmarks interactively specimen photographs (point--click) returns result directly, following either FISHMORPH scheme generic one. Align configurations Generalised Procrustes Analysis: gpa_fish(). Derive traits: inter-landmark linear distances (linear_distances()) normalised ecomorphological ratios (morpho_ratios()); correct allometry correct_allometry(). Explore shape: build plot morphological space morpho_space(). default, plot() shows group individual points, dashed segments linking group mean, group mean , 95% dispersion ellipse (style = "spider"); style = "hull" recovers classical convex-hull display, style = "density" draws non-parametric kernel-density contour instead ellipse, groups whose points visibly skewed multimodal. group legend drawn just outside plot box default (legend_position = "outside") never overlaps data; pass standard legend() keyword (e.g. "topright") previous, inside--box placement. style/legend_position options available plot.intrait_traitspace() (trait_space()). Assess variability error: intraspecific morphological variability (intraspecific_variability(), combining shape disparity coefficients variation), measurement error / repeatability replicated digitization (measurement_error()), landmark-level digitization (operator) bias (digitization_error(), following protocol Boutic, 2026), interspecific/intraspecific (ITV) partitioning trait variance (itv_index(), optionally split - within-population components). Additional utilities: plot_landmarks() detect_outliers() quality control digitization (visual inspection automatic Procrustes-distance-based outlier screening, respectively), summary_traits() tidy group-level summaries, trait_disparity() permutation tests group differences functional trait dispersion. plot_landmarks() plot_fishmorph_points() accept background_image (.jpg/.jpeg .png photograph specimen), overlaying digitized landmarks directly original photograph visual quality control — e.g. spotting landmark placed body outline (requires jpeg png package, matching image format).

title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: manual digitization workflows produce “wide” spreadsheet: one row per specimen (per replicate digitization), one pair columns per landmark (e.g. X_1, Y_1, X_2, Y_2, ..., 1_X, 1_Y, 2_X, 2_Y, ...), rather “long”/tidy layout expected read_landmarks_csv(). read_landmarks_xlsx() reads layout directly .xlsx/.xls file reshapes "intrait_landmarks" object, without hand-written reshaping code – whatever exact column-naming convention (x_pattern/y_pattern) number landmarks (n_landmarks) involved. also optionally joins second, one-row-per-specimen spreadsheet identifications metadata directly (species_file): replicate-digitization sheet (several digitizations per specimen, one row – e.g. measurement_error()/digitization_error()) works way, just different x_pattern/y_pattern id_cols: deliberately generic import: coordinate cells blank contain literal text "NA" become NA silently, anything else non-numeric triggers warning naming offending column(s), species_file performs plain left-join species_by (attempt resolve conflicting uncertain identifications – R first identification sheet needs ). generalised, reusable version private cleaning script originally written import real T-26 Saudrune field data (data-raw/t26_saudrune_prepare.R), usable directly new field season survey column-naming convention landmark count. Requires readxl package (Suggested, installed default).
code: fish <- read_landmarks_xlsx(   "measurements.xlsx", sheet = "Principal", n_landmarks = 21,   id_cols = c("Code", "Operator"),   species_file = "identifications.xlsx", species_by = "Code" ) biais <- read_landmarks_xlsx(   "measurements.xlsx", sheet = "Repeatability", n_landmarks = 21,   x_pattern = "{i}_X", y_pattern = "{i}_Y",   id_cols = c("Code", "Replicate") )

title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: set specimens digitized several times (replicated landmarking photographs), digitization_error() quantifies dispersed repeated landmark placements around consensus position, normalised bias comparable across specimens different sizes, aggregated hierarchically landmark individual, species, overall community bias: reproduces protocol used L. Boutic (internship report, 2026, CRBE/INTRAIT) quantify operator bias French Guiana freshwater fish landmark digitization: deliberately operates raw digitized coordinates (Procrustes superimposition) bias can attributed individual landmarks (e.g. flag ones need stricter operational definition), complementing overall, rotation/scale-invariant repeatability estimate measurement_error(..., method = "procrustes").
code: fish <- simulate_fish_landmarks(n_per_species = 8, n_replicates = 10) individual_id <- sub("_rep[0-9]+$", "", rownames(fish$metadata)) derr <- digitization_error(fish, individual = individual_id) derr plot(derr)  # bias by landmark, ordered by increasing median bias

what: Landmark quality control: orientation and geometry correction
title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: Generalised Procrustes Analysis, digitized configuration geometrically consistent state: every specimen left-right -orientation, every landmark placed according conventions expected digitization protocol (e.g. given set points share common X Y coordinate). Procrustes superimposition (gpa_fish()) removes translation, rotation scale, fix mirrored specimen landmark placed protocol’s conventions – either can silently distort resulting shape space exactly undetected outlier . intraitR provides small set functions detect correct issues, documented reproducible way, alignment: impute_landmarks() estimates small number missing landmarks (e.g. commonly missing landmark 5 FISHMORPH scheme) rest configuration sample consensus, specimen simply dropped one incomplete point. standardize_orientation() detects corrects specimens digitized inconsistent orientation, enforcing consistent head-left, belly-frame across whole sample. correct_landmarks(rule = "check_geometry") audits fixed battery geometric conventions expected digitization protocol (e.g. FISHMORPH scheme) returns diagnostic report, without modifying anything; correct_geometry() automatically snaps every flagged landmark shared coordinate implied trusted neighbours. correct_landmarks(rule = "align") lets single, visually identified point corrected manually instead. Every one functions records changed shared, mergeable audit-trail attribute (attr(x$coords, "corrected") attr(x$coords, "correction_log")), full correction history accumulates across successive calls object, plot_landmarks() / plot_fishmorph_points() highlight imputed corrected points visually (red blue, respectively) review. complete pipeline raw landmarks functional trait space, including quality-control step, looks like : corrected, oriented, geometry-fixed object (fish_fixed) also feeds directly fishmorph_shape_landmarks() gpa_fish() shape-space side workflow (see demo("pipeline_T26_saudrune")).
code: fish          <- load_t26_saudrune_landmarks() fish_imputed  <- impute_landmarks(fish) fish_oriented <- standardize_orientation(fish_imputed) geom_check    <- correct_landmarks(fish_imputed, rule = "check_geometry") fish_fixed    <- correct_geometry(fish_oriented)  # visually compare a specimen before/after geometry correction, with any # flagged convention highlighted in orange: plot_fishmorph_points(fish_oriented, specimen = "T-26-0024_Operator_1",                        geometry_check = geom_check) plot_fishmorph_points(fish_fixed, specimen = "T-26-0024_Operator_1",                        geometry_check = geom_check)  segments_fixed <- fishmorph_segments(fish_fixed) ratios_fixed   <- fishmorph_ratios(segments_fixed)  ts_fixed <- trait_space(   ratios_fixed, groups = fish$metadata$species,   na_action = "omit", remove_outliers = TRUE ) # flags any within-species outliers found, see ts_fixed$outlier_screen plot(ts_fixed)

title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: intraitR also implements specific digitization trait protocol FISHMORPH database (Brosse et al., 2021, Global Ecology Biogeography), based fixed scheme 21 (optionally 22) landmarks per specimen: fishmorph_segments() computes 11 linear measurements protocol (Bl, Bd, Hd, Eh, Mo, PFi, PFl, Ed, Jl, CPd, CFd) directly digitized points, converting pixels centimetres using embedded scale bar. fishmorph_ratios() computes 9 unitless FISHMORPH ratios (BEl, VEp, REs, OGp, RMl, BLs, PFv, PFs, CPt), including special-case rules Villéger et al. (2010). trait_space() builds PCA/PCoA functional trait space numeric trait table (FISHMORPH ratios otherwise), default applying log10(x + 1) transformation followed centring/scaling unit variance ordination (log_transform = TRUE, scale = TRUE); plot() uses spider/ellipse display morpho_space(). trait_disparity() tests whether groups differ multivariate dispersion functional traits (trait variance, .e. trace group’s trait covariance matrix), permutation group labels, computed full standardised trait matrix rather two axes retained plotting. trait_space(na_action = ...) handles missing trait values: "omit", "impute_mean", "impute_group_mean", "missforest" (nonparametric random-forest imputation via missForest package, using groups auxiliary predictor). plot_fishmorph_points() simulate_fishmorph_points() visualise simulate data following 21/22-point scheme. bootstrap_functional_space() quantifies much representing species real individuals, rather centroid, inflates estimated functional richness PCA space, following bootstrap procedure Bertrand (2026). method selects richness measure: "convexhull" (default, n-dimensional convex-hull volume; requires geometry package, Suggested, installed default), "dendrogram" (UPGMA functional dendrogram branch length, extra package needed), "tpd" (Trait Probability Density; TPD package), "hypervolume" (Gaussian-kernel hypervolume; hypervolume package). species_sensitivity() complements per-species index (Bertrand, 2026), method choice: species, centroid replaced individuals turn (every species fixed centroid), resulting percent change functional richness summarised mean effect min-max range, identifying species drive ITV’s expansion functional space. compare_functional_richness() runs bootstrap_functional_space() across several methods tabulates fd_ref, fd_boot_mean, percent change, bootstrap p-value side side, triangulating whether ITV-driven expansion functional space robust choice richness measure. method whose package missing, errors, recorded skipped rather failing whole comparison.

title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: bootstrap_functional_space(), species_sensitivity(), trait_disparity() repeat independent, expensive computation many times: n_boot bootstrap draws, one convex-hull recomputation per individual, iter label permutations, respectively. Two things worth knowing applying large panel (Bertrand, 2026’s regional data set, instance, 1,302 individuals across 55 species): Convex-hull cost grows quickly dimension. three functions rely geometry::convhulln() (Qhull), whose worst-case complexity n points d dimensions  (McMullen, 1970, upper bound theorem maximum number facets d-dimensional polytope). practice means default 2-3 retained PCA axes (n_axes, var_threshold-based automatic choice) stay fast even hundreds species, requesting much higher n_axes species-rich data set can make individual hull computation noticeably slower — worth keeping mind increasing n_axes much beyond var_threshold already selects. repeated computation independent others, three functions distribute loop across worker processes via (Suggested) future.apply package installed, zero configuration required package : set plan per session future, e.g. plan() set, without future.apply installed, three functions run exactly (plain sequential loop), purely opt-speed-— results identical either way, since parallel draws use future.seed = TRUE (L’Ecuyer-CMRG streams) reproducible random number generation across workers.
code: future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 4) bfs <- bootstrap_functional_space(ts, n_boot = 2000)  # now parallelised future::plan(future::sequential)  # back to single-core when done

title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: See vignette("intraitR-intro") full walkthrough.
code: library(intraitR)  fish <- simulate_fish_landmarks(n_per_species = 15, n_replicates = 3) gpa  <- gpa_fish(fish)  distances <- list(SL = c(1, 7), BD = c(3, 10)) ratios    <- morpho_ratios(fish, distances, norm_by = "SL")  ms <- morpho_space(gpa, groups = fish$metadata$species) plot(ms)  intraspecific_variability(   gpa = gpa, groups = fish$metadata$species,   traits = ratios[, "BD_ratio", drop = FALSE] )

what: Real data: the T-26 La Saudrune data set
title: intraitR: Morphological Trait Analysis Toolkit for Freshwater Fish
text: Beyond simulated examples, intraitR ships real landmark data set electric-fishing survey Saudrune (Adour-Garonne basin, France): 279 fish 8 species, digitized two independent operators FISHMORPH scheme, plus 25-fish x 9-10-replicate intra-operator repeatability trial. See ?load_t26_saudrune data demo("pipeline_T26_saudrune") complete worked pipeline (import, GPA, quality control, FISHMORPH traits, trait space, itv_index(), measurement_error(), digitization_error(), trait_disparity()) applied end end. FISHMORPH-specific workflow, load_t26_saudrune_landmarks() returns data directly "intrait_landmarks" object, exactly format produced simulate_fishmorph_points() — drop-real-data replacement used throughout package’s examples: Operator identity anonymised shipped data ("Operator_1", "Operator_2" rather real names recorded field spreadsheets). loaders take operator argument restrict single operator’s digitizations — natural way build two separate functional trait spaces, one per operator, check whether results sensitive digitizing: operator modular: passed table operator column, ignored warning (rows returned) rather raising error. load_t26_saudrune("operators") "repeatability" landmark tables keyed code, species — species identity lives separate "identifications" table design (landmark measurement never needs know species, identifications can revised independently coordinates). Pass species = TRUE join species/id_status back onto either table code:
code: ops <- load_t26_saudrune("operators") lm  <- read_landmarks_csv(ops) fish <- load_t26_saudrune_landmarks() fishmorph_ratios(fishmorph_segments(fish)) fish_op1 <- load_t26_saudrune_landmarks(operator = "Operator_1") fish_op2 <- load_t26_saudrune_landmarks(operator = "Operator_2") ops <- load_t26_saudrune("operators", species = TRUE) table(ops$species)

title: Bootstrap-based estimate of functional space volume from individual data — bootstrap_functional_space
text: Compares functional richness obtained species represented real individuals functional richness obtained species collapsed centroid (mean trait position), PCA-based trait space, following bootstrap procedure Bertrand (2026, Section "Bootstrap-based functional space estimates"). n_boot bootstrap "communities", one individual drawn random per species functional richness individual-level points computed (fd_boot); distribution compared single centroid-based reference richness (fd_ref), obtained replacing species mean position individuals recomputing richness measure. single randomly chosen individual necessarily sits somewhere within (edge ) species' dispersion, fd_boot expected equal exceed fd_ref whenever species show non-trivial intraspecific trait variability (ITV); function also tests whether fd_ref sits unusually low relative bootstrap distribution (see Details).

title: Bootstrap-based estimate of functional space volume from individual data — bootstrap_functional_space
text: x Either object class "intrait_traitspace" (trait_space(), built groups supplied), data.frame/matrix numeric traits (one row per individual), case groups must also supplied log_transform/scale preprocessing trait_space() applied PCA described . groups Required x raw trait table (one value per individual, typically species identity); ignored (taken x$groups) x "intrait_traitspace" object. method Character, functional richness measure compute PCA-based trait space fd_ref every fd_boot draw. One : "convexhull" (default) n-dimensional convex-hull volume (Villeger, Mason & Mouillot, 2008), via geometry::convhulln() (Qhull; Barber, Dobkin & Huhdanpaa, 1996) – measure used Bertrand (2026) one strictly requires nlevels(groups) > n_axes (see n_axes). Requires (Suggested) geometry package. "dendrogram" Total branch length UPGMA functional dendrogram (Petchey & Gaston, 2002), via stats::hclust(). distance-based (non-volumetric) alternative needs additional Suggested package places restriction number species relative n_axes. "tpd" Functional richness Trait Probability Density (Carmona, de Bello, Mason & Leps, 2019), via TPD::TPDsMean()/TPD::TPDc()/TPD::REND(): species represented fixed-bandwidth Gaussian kernel rather single point, FRichness proportion shared evaluation grid occupied union kernels. Requires (Suggested) TPD package. "hypervolume" Gaussian-kernel hypervolume (Blonder et al., 2014, 2018), via hypervolume::hypervolume_gaussian()/ hypervolume::get_volume(): conceptually similar "tpd" (smoothed, kernel-based volume rather hard-edged hull), estimated Monte Carlo sampling rather fixed grid, far computationally expensive four – consider smaller n_boot (e.g. 20-50) /smaller hv_samples_per_point methods. Requires (Suggested) hypervolume package. "tpd" "hypervolume" represent species (bootstrap-drawn individual) kernel rather bare point, using bandwidth computed full individual-level PCA scores reused, unchanged, fd_ref every fd_boot draw (see Details fixed-bandwidth/fixed-grid design required draws comparable ). n_axes Integer, number PCA axes retain. NULL (default), smallest number axes whose cumulative proportion variance reaches var_threshold used automatically – Bertrand (2026) used 8 axes, capturing 98% total variance, similar ten-trait morphological data set. method = "convexhull" , must leave strictly species axes (nlevels(groups) > n_axes), since non-degenerate n-dimensional convex hull requires least n_axes + 1 affinely independent points; lower n_axes (var_threshold) satisfied. methods need get warning instead. var_threshold Cumulative proportion variance used automatically choose n_axes n_axes = NULL. Defaults 0.98, Bertrand (2026). n_boot Integer, number bootstrap "communities". , one individual drawn random (independently across species) every species, functional richness resulting one-individual-per-species point set computed. also number draws significance test based (see Details), larger values give finer-grained p-value floor 1 / (n_boot + 1). Defaults 100, Bertrand (2026). log_transform, scale trait_space(); used x raw trait table (ignored, taken x, x "intrait_traitspace" object). dendrogram_linkage Character, clustering method passed stats::hclust(method =) method = "dendrogram". Defaults "average" (UPGMA), Petchey & Gaston (2002). tpd_alpha Numeric, greater 0 1, passed TPD::TPDsMean()'s alpha method = "tpd": proportion species' kernel probability mass included. Defaults 0.95 (TPD package's default). tpd_bw_factor Numeric, method = "tpd", fixed per-axis kernel standard deviation tpd_bw_factor times axis's overall (-species) standard deviation across full individual-level PCA scores – plug-bandwidth, since single bootstrap-drawn individual carries within-species variance estimate kernel (see Details). Defaults 0.5. tpd_n_divisions Passed TPD::TPDsMean()'s n_divisions method = "tpd" (grid resolution); NULL (default) uses function's default. hv_bw_method Character, method passed hypervolume::estimate_bandwidth() method = "hypervolume". Defaults "silverman". hv_samples_per_point Integer, passed hypervolume::hypervolume_gaussian()'s samples.per.point method = "hypervolume". Defaults conservative 500 (rather function's dimensionality-scaled default), since value paid n_boot times ; increase precise (slower) estimate. composition Optional numeric matrix data.frame giving species composition one communities/sites: one row per community, one column per species, column names matching species labels used groups (.e. levels(factor(groups))). Entries can presence/absence (0/1) abundance – either way, whether entry greater 0 used (abundance values otherwise weight computation; see Details). Row names, present, used community identifiers (otherwise "community_1", "community_2", ...). Columns matching species groups dropped warning. supplied, exact reference-vs-bootstrap principle described repeated independently community, restricted community's species (see Details); results returned $communities (see Return) addition , instead , whole-species-pool fd_ref/fd_boot . Defaults NULL (per-community computation).

what: Compare bootstrap-based functional richness estimates across methods — compare_functional_richness
title: Compare bootstrap-based functional richness estimates across methods — compare_functional_richness
text: Runs bootstrap_functional_space() per requested method ("convexhull", "dendrogram", "tpd", "hypervolume") data, tabulates results side side. Convex-hull volume, dendrogram branch length, TPD richness, hypervolume scale, raw fd_ref/fd_boot_mean values directly comparable across rows; comparable whether method agrees, qualitatively, individual-based richness exceeds centroid-based reference, roughly much relative (percentage) terms – exactly triangulation function .

title: Compare bootstrap-based functional richness estimates across methods — compare_functional_richness
code: compare_functional_richness(   x,   groups = NULL,   methods = c("convexhull", "dendrogram", "tpd", "hypervolume"),   n_axes = NULL,   var_threshold = 0.98,   n_boot = 100,   log_transform = TRUE,   scale = TRUE,   alpha = 0.05,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Compare bootstrap-based functional richness estimates across methods — compare_functional_richness
text: x, groups, n_axes, var_threshold, n_boot, log_transform, scale bootstrap_functional_space(). methods Character vector, one "convexhull", "dendrogram", "tpd", "hypervolume" (see bootstrap_functional_space() measures). Defaults four. method whose Suggested package installed, errors reason (e.g. n_axes large "convexhull"'s affine-independence requirement), fatal: recorded status != "ok" $summary NA numeric columns, comparison proceeds remaining methods. alpha Numeric, significance threshold used flag $summary$significant summarised print(). Defaults 0.05. Purely display/summary convenience: underlying p_value method always reported full. seed Optional single integer. supplied, set.seed(seed) called immediately method's bootstrap_functional_space() call, every method draws sequence one-individual-per- species bootstrap "communities" (community 1 draw every method, community 2 every method, ) – useful want per-draw richness values directly paired across methods (e.g. correlate ), rather merely comparing summary statistics. Defaults NULL (explicit seeding; method's draws continue ambient RNG stream, single ordinary call bootstrap_functional_space() ). ... method-specific tuning arguments forwarded -every bootstrap_functional_space() call: dendrogram_linkage, tpd_alpha, tpd_bw_factor, tpd_n_divisions, hv_bw_method, hv_samples_per_point. Irrelevant arguments simply ignored whichever method use .

title: Compare bootstrap-based functional richness estimates across methods — compare_functional_richness
text: object class "intrait_richness_comparison", list elements: summary (data.frame, one row per requested method, order methods, columns method, status ("ok" "skipped: <error message>" note), n_axes, var_explained, fd_ref, fd_boot_mean, fd_boot_sd, diff, pct_diff (100 * diff / fd_ref, cross-method-comparable quantity), p_value, significant (p_value < alpha); NA every numeric column skipped method), results (named list full "intrait_bootstrap_fspace" object every method succeeded, e.g. plot()-ing individual method's bootstrap histogram), n_boot, alpha. dedicated print() plot() methods.

title: Compare bootstrap-based functional richness estimates across methods — compare_functional_richness
text: method gets , independent call bootstrap_functional_space() – including fresh PCA n_boot bootstrap draws – rather sharing internal computation across methods; keeps comparison exactly trustworthy calling bootstrap_functional_space() directly four times, cost repeating (comparatively cheap) PCA step. Use seed specifically want draws reused across methods rather four independent bootstrap samples. pct_diff one quantity meaningfully compared across rows: consistent, similarly sized, statistically significant pct_diff across methods stronger evidence intraspecific trait variability genuinely inflates estimated functional space single method's result taken alone, since four measures make different assumptions (hard-edged hull vs. kernel-smoothed density vs. distance-based dendrogram) unlikely agree spuriously reason.

what: Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry — correct_allometry
title: Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry — correct_allometry
text: Removes component shape variation linearly associated size (allometry) regressing Procrustes shape coordinates log centroid size retaining residuals, re-expressed shape coordinates sample's mean size. useful comparing shape among specimens species differ substantially body size, subsequent morphospace disparity analyses simply driven size-related shape change.

title: Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry — correct_allometry
code: correct_allometry(gpa, method = c("common", "group"), groups = NULL)

title: Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry — correct_allometry
text: gpa object class "intrait_gpa", returned gpa_fish(). method Character, one "common" (single common allometric trajectory fitted across specimens) "group" (allometric trajectories allowed differ group, e.g. species; requires species column gpa$metadata, groups argument). groups Optional factor (character vector) group membership, required method = "group" gpa$metadata species column.

title: Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry — correct_allometry
text: p x k x n array allometry-corrected shape coordinates, dimnames gpa$coords.

title: Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry — correct_allometry
text: implements "common allometric component" correction described Mosimann (1970) widely used geometric morphometrics literature (e.g. Adams & Nistri, 2010): shape regressed log centroid size, residuals added back value predicted mean log centroid size, yielding size-standardised shape every specimen. simplification intended exploratory use; formal hypothesis testing allometric trajectories, use geomorph::procD.lm() directly.

title: Correct Procrustes shape coordinates for allometry — correct_allometry
text: Mosimann JE (1970). Size allometry: size shape variables characterizations lognormal generalized gamma distributions. Journal American Statistical Association, 65(330), 930-945. Adams DC, Nistri (2010). Ontogenetic convergence evolution foot morphology European cave salamanders. BMC Evolutionary Biology, 10, 216.

what: Standardize landmark configurations to a common scale, orientation, and geometric convention — correct_geometry
title: Standardize landmark configurations to a common scale, orientation, and geometric convention — correct_geometry
text: full geometric standardization pipeline FISHMORPH landmark configurations, applied every specimen fixed order: (1) rescale body landmarks isotropically fit within [0, 1] (preserving body shape – see Details); (2) reposition embedded scale bar (landmarks 20-21) fixed corner [0, 1] space, clean horizontal reference segment rather wherever happened sit original photograph; (3) rotate body main axis (landmarks 1-2) exactly horizontal, landmark 1 always left landmark 2, shift vertically axis sits Y = 0.5 every specimen; (4) correct, exactly correct_landmarks(rule = "check_geometry") audits, whichever five landmark-coordinate-scatter conventions still fails hold specimen canonical frame. step operates output step , order, every specimen.

title: Standardize landmark configurations to a common scale, orientation, and geometric convention — correct_geometry
code: correct_geometry(   landmarks,   specimen = NULL,   scale_bar_pos = c(0.1, 0.1),   tolerance_coord = 1e-06 )

title: Standardize landmark configurations to a common scale, orientation, and geometric convention — correct_geometry
text: landmarks object class "intrait_landmarks", raw p x k x n landmark array, least 21 landmarks FISHMORPH scheme (points 1-19 plus scale bar, 20-21). specimen NULL (default) standardize every specimen, integer/character vector restrict subset. scale_bar_pos Numeric length-2 vector, c(x, y) position (post-rescaling [0, 1] space) landmark 20 moved ; landmark 21 placed right, scale_bar_pos + c(length, 0), length scale bar's original length scaled factor body (see Details) – calibration ratio scale bar body preserved, position orientation standardized. Defaults c(0.1, 0.1) (bottom-left). tolerance_coord Numeric, proportion body length (Bl, distance landmarks 1 2, now measured rescaled rotated frame) point landmark group allowed sit shared coordinate rest group agrees , corrected step (4) – meaning correct_landmarks(rule = "check_geometry")'s tolerance_coord, much smaller default (1e-6, .e. essentially visible misalignment corrected): correct_landmarks(rule = "check_geometry")'s 0.02 default tuned diagnostic threshold (distinguishing routine digitization noise problem worth human's attention), whereas function's job actually produce clean, standardized configuration, principled reason leave detectable misalignment uncorrected. Raise (e.g. match check_geometry()'s 0.02) preserve small deviations consider acceptable digitization noise instead correcting .

title: Manually correct a misplaced landmark using an alignment rule — correct_landmarks
text: Applies documented, reproducible correction one landmark coordinates single specimen, visual quality-control (e.g. plot_fishmorph_points()) identified misplaced point – rather editing digitized coordinates hand outside package, leaving record changed . Currently implements one rule, "align": landmark groups expected, digitization protocol, share X Y coordinate (e.g. points 9, 8, 11, 4, ventral reference line drawn plot_fishmorph_points()'s outline); one visibly , correct_landmarks() snaps specified point(s) median position , trusted points group – never silently decides point wrong.

title: Manually correct a misplaced landmark using an alignment rule — correct_landmarks
text: rule = "align" computes reference value median (mean) setdiff(points, correct)'s axis coordinate, robustness one trusted reference points also happens slightly ; reference points (e.g. two), offers little protection correction visually re-checked afterwards. specimen modified; every specimen's landmarks left untouched. impute_landmarks(), substitute re-digitizing original photograph possible. rule = "check_geometry" runs fixed set seven checks, two different kinds: Five checks ask whether landmark group shares raw coordinate FISHMORPH protocol expects (unit = "rel_bl", gated tolerance_coord; identical criterion correct_geometry() uses decide correct): (1)-(3) segments (1, 9), (3, 4), (10, 11) share common X (.e. vertical, perpendicular main body axis) – reported perpendicular_seg_1_9_vs_axis, perpendicular_seg_3_4_vs_axis, perpendicular_seg_10_11_vs_axis; (4) eye-socket line (5, 13, 7, 14, 6, 8) likewise share common X, reported perpendicular_eye_vertical_vs_axis; (5) ventral line (9, 8, 11, 4) share common Y (.e. horizontal, parallel main axis), reported axis_horizontal_parallel. , deviation absolute difference deviant point's coordinate shared reference value point(s) agree (median, two multi-point groups; fixed anatomical anchor, three two-point segments – see correct_geometry()'s Details ), expressed proportion body length (Bl, distance landmarks 1 2) default tolerance meaningful across specimens/data sets digitized different scales. Two checks remain angular (unit = "deg", gated tolerance), compare orientation two lines rather asking whether single group points shares coordinate, deliberately sensitive photograph oriented: (6) eye_axis_vertical_alignment, whether eye-socket line's best-fit orientation close vertical image's frame (.e. photograph looks reasonably level – can, , fail validly measured visibly rotated photograph, excluded fishmorph_segments()'s geometry_check trait-NA-ing); (7) parallel_vertical_segments, whether segments (1,9), (3,4), (10,11), eye-socket line mutually parallel (reported single largest pairwise deviation among ). line's orientation, two checks, estimated first principal axis (via stats::prcomp()), reduces exact two-point direction two-landmark segments robust fit longer, multi-landmark lines. what: Detect potential digitization outliers from a GPA-aligned sample — detect_outliers
title: Detect potential digitization outliers from a GPA-aligned sample — detect_outliers
text: Flags specimens whose Procrustes distance sample consensus shape unusually large, fast, general-purpose quality-control screen landmark digitization errors (mislabelled points, landmarks digitized order, gross measurement mistakes), spirit exploratory outlier screening implemented geomorph::plotOutliers().

title: Detect potential digitization outliers from a GPA-aligned sample — detect_outliers
code: detect_outliers(gpa, threshold = 3, plot = TRUE)  # S3 method for class 'intrait_outliers' print(x, ...)

title: Detect potential digitization outliers from a GPA-aligned sample — detect_outliers
text: gpa object class "intrait_gpa" (gpa_fish()). threshold Numeric, number median absolute deviations (MAD) median Procrustes distance beyond specimen flagged. Defaults 3. median MAD used, rather mean standard deviation, robust outliers screened . plot Logical, draw ordered dot plot Procrustes distances flagged specimens highlighted threshold marked dashed line. Defaults TRUE. x object class "intrait_outliers", returned detect_outliers(). ... Currently unused.

title: Detect potential digitization outliers from a GPA-aligned sample — detect_outliers
text: object class "intrait_outliers", list elements procrustes_distance (named numeric vector, one value per specimen), threshold_value (numeric Procrustes-distance cut-implied threshold), outliers (character vector flagged specimen names), rank (data.frame columns specimen, procrustes_distance, outlier, ordered least atypical). dedicated print method; plot = TRUE, base R plot also drawn side effect. Invisibly returns x.

title: Detect potential digitization outliers from a GPA-aligned sample — detect_outliers
text: coarse, univariate screen based overall Procrustes distance consensus shape, intended fast first pass rather definitive statistical test: genuinely unusual correctly digitized specimen (e.g. naturally extreme morphology) also flagged, digitization error affecting subset landmarks way partly cancels overall Procrustes distance missed. Always visually inspect flagged specimens (e.g. plot_landmarks() plot_fishmorph_points()) excluding downstream analyses.
