title: Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis — phylo_pcoa
text: Derives quantitative "phylogenetic trait" axes phylogenetic tree, Principal Coordinates Analysis (PCoA) tree's patristic (cophenetic) distances among species. resulting axes summarise species' overall phylogenetic position small set numeric variables, species-plus-axes data.frame deliberately shaped can passed directly traits argument trait_space() – letting build "phylogenetic space" exactly ordination machinery used morphological trait spaces elsewhere package, compare functional phylogenetic diversity loss (e.g. bootstrap_functional_space() species_sensitivity()) using statistics .

title: Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis — phylo_pcoa
code: phylo_pcoa(   tree,   species = NULL,   k = NULL,   correction = c("none", "cailliez", "lingoes"),   ultrametric = TRUE,   ultrametric_method = c("nnls", "extend") )  # S3 method for class 'intrait_phylopcoa' print(x, ...)

title: Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis — phylo_pcoa
text: tree object class "phylo" (e.g. ape::read.tree(), ape::read.nexus(), bundled load_fishmorph_phylogeny()), tip labels matching species names used elsewhere analysis (e.g. fish$metadata$species, spaces, underscores, dots treated interchangeable – see Details). species Optional character vector species retain (typically actual species pool, e.g. levels(factor(fish$metadata$species))). Species found among tree$tip.label dropped warning; defaults tree$tip.label. k Integer, number phylogenetic axes keep. Defaults axes (corrected, correction != "none") positive eigenvalue. correction Character, "none" (default), "cailliez", "lingoes" – passed ape::pcoa()'s correction argument. Patristic/cophenetic distances guaranteed perfectly Euclidean, can produce handful small negative eigenvalues decomposition; "cailliez" "lingoes" add constant distances remove (see ?ape::pcoa). Legendre & Anderson (1999) found Cailliez correction can inflate Type error downstream permutation tests, treat "lingoes" conservative choice correction needed test; "none" simply drops negative-eigenvalue axes (ape::pcoa() default). ultrametric Logical, coerce tree ultrametric (constant root--tip distance) computing distances, using phytools::force.ultrametric(), already (tolerance; see ape::.ultrametric()). Defaults TRUE, matching time-calibrated phylogeny's assumption; set FALSE tree's branch lengths meant represent strict molecular clock (e.g. phylogram substitutions/site) cophenetic distance still appropriate summary purposes. ultrametric_method Character, "nnls" (default) "extend", passed phytools::force.ultrametric()'s method argument ultrametric = TRUE tree already (numerically) ultrametric; see ?phytools::force.ultrametric – numerical adjustment representing tree, re-estimation divergence times, reported message() triggered. x object class "intrait_phylopcoa", returned phylo_pcoa(). ... Currently unused.

title: Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis — phylo_pcoa
text: object class "intrait_phylopcoa", list elements traits (data.frame, one row per species, species column k numeric columns PCoA1, PCoA2, ... – ready pass traits trait_space(), auto-detects groups species column), var_explained (percent variance explained retained axis), k, correction, tree (pruned, possibly ultrametric-coerced, tree actually used), dropped_species (species entries found tree$tip.label). Invisibly returns x.

title: Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis — phylo_pcoa
text: Species names matched tree$tip.label collapsing run spaces, underscores, /dots single underscore (various separator conventions used different tip-label/species sources – e.g. "Barbus_barbus", "Barbus.barbus", bundled load_fishmorph_phylogeny() tree), species = "Barbus barbus" matches tip labelled "Barbus.barbus" without requiring caller reformat names first. least 3 matched species required meaningful ordination. function deliberately covers generic, reusable step turning tree plus species list ordination axes – attempt taxonomic name resolution, tree sourcing, trait/occurrence data assembly, specific wherever tree species list come scope package.

title: Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis — phylo_pcoa
text: Cailliez, F. (1983). analytical solution additive constant problem. Psychometrika, 48, 305-308. doi:10.1007/BF02294026 Lingoes, J. C. (1971). boundary conditions monotone analysis symmetric matrices. Psychometrika, 36, 195-203. doi:10.1007/BF02291398 Legendre, P., & Anderson, M. J. (1999). Distance-based redundancy analysis: testing multispecies responses multifactorial ecological experiments. Ecological Monographs, 69(1), 1-24. doi:10.1890/0012-9615(1999)069[0001:DBRATM]2.0.CO;2

title: Phylogenetic Principal Coordinates Analysis — phylo_pcoa
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("ape", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(1)   tree <- ape::rcoal(8, tip.label = paste0("sp_", 1:8))   pp <- phylo_pcoa(tree, k = 3)   pp    # use directly as `traits` in trait_space(): groups auto-detected from   # the `species` column, exactly like a morphological trait matrix   ts_phylo <- trait_space(pp$traits, na_action = "fail", log_transform = FALSE)   plot(ts_phylo) } #> Warning: Dropping non-numeric column(s) from the ordination: species #> flag_outliers: 8 group(s) have fewer than outlier_min_n = 5 specimens and were not screened (distance still reported, flagged = NA).  # }

Instead specimen, select every digitization belonging given fish, matched landmarks$metadata$individual – identifier used throughout package link digitizations fish (see digitization_error(), measurement_error(); load_t26_saudrune_landmarks() identical code column). useful landmarks holds one row per specimen/operator specimen/replicate combination (e.g. load_t26_saudrune_landmarks("operators"), two rows per fish, one per operator) natural look fish code exact specimen identifier. individual matches one specimen (e.g. two operators, several replicate digitizations), matches plotted side side single figure (one panel per specimen, titled specimen identifier) can compared visually; background_image ignored (warning), since single photograph assumed apply every match. Requires landmarks$metadata individual column. Defaults NULL (use specimen instead). labels Logical, label landmarks index. Defaults TRUE. legend Logical, draw legend measurement names/colours. Defaults TRUE. legend_position One "outside" (default: drawn margin, just right plot box, never overlaps fish outline) standard graphics::legend() position keyword (e.g. "topright") draw inside plot box instead, previous versions. background_image Optional path .jpg/.jpeg .png photograph specimen, drawn background layer beneath landmarks measurement segments (e.g. visually check whether landmark placed body outline). meaningful original, un-aligned digitized coordinates. Requires (Suggested) jpeg package .jpg/.jpeg files, png .png files. Defaults NULL (background). flip_y Logical, flip background_image vertically plotting, match bottom-left-origin convention digitized landmark coordinates top-row-first convention image files (see flip_y plot_landmarks()). Ignored background_image NULL. Defaults TRUE. outline Logical, add set purely visual reference lines, drawn addition (visually subordinate ) 11 coloured measurement segments, reproducing digitization protocol sheet: body outline (solid, points 1-5-3-16-18-19-17-4-6, closed back 1), horizontal reference line along belly (points 9-8-11-4), vertical reference line eye level (points 5-13-7-14-6-8), eye (circle centred point 7 diameter equal Ed measurement, .e. distance points 13 14). body outline drawn plain solid line; two reference lines eye circle drawn light dotted, read background guides rather measurements. landmark missing (NA) specimen silently dropped reference paths rather leaving gap – e.g. real T-26 specimens commonly missing landmark 5, case body outline falls back direct 1-3 segment. Defaults TRUE. highlight_imputed Logical, colour landmark points red carry "imputed" marker specimen – .e. estimated impute_landmarks() rather digitized – instead usual grey, matching "Imputed landmark" legend entry. visible effect landmarks without marker (e.g. never run impute_landmarks()). Defaults TRUE. highlight_corrected Logical, colour landmark points blue carry "corrected" marker specimen – .e. manually adjusted correct_landmarks() – matching "Corrected landmark" legend entry. point imputed corrected, blue (corrected) takes precedence. visible effect landmarks without marker. Defaults TRUE. geometry_check Optional object class "intrait_geometry_check", returned correct_landmarks(landmarks, rule = "check_geometry") – typically computed whole data set reused across plot calls, rather recomputed . supplied, landmark implicated check failed (ok = FALSE) specimen coloured orange, matching "Geometry check flagged" legend entry, specimens worth closer look (correct_landmarks() fix) stand visually. NULL (default) draws highlighting. Ignored (effect) geometry_check row specimen. highlight_geometry Logical, whether apply geometry_check highlighting described . relevant geometry_check supplied. Defaults TRUE. scale_unit Character, real-world unit digitization scale bar (landmarks 20-21) represents one (e.g. "mm", "cm", "dm", "m", unit label) – FISHMORPH protocol's standard calibration segment 1 cm, hence default "cm"; change data set digitized different real-world unit. Set NULL omit bar's text label entirely (bar still drawn). label built automatically "1 <scale_unit> = <length>", <length> specimen's digitized distance landmarks 20 21, always reflects actual calibration length used specimen rather fixed, possibly inaccurate, caption. drawn landmarks 20 21 present (non-NA) specimen. bar drawn solid, filled bar border (thin open line), low , schematically near plot's origin (bottom-left corner) rather landmarks 20/21's true digitized coordinates – can otherwise land anywhere frame, even fish – still drawn real digitized length (.e. still true scale); caption placed directly . Neither landmark individually number-labelled marked point symbol. axis_range Either "auto" (default) numeric vector length 2, c(min, max), shared x y. "auto" uses c(0, 1), clean, round tick labels, whenever every coordinate specimen within generous +/-15% (normalised convention used throughout package's examples T-26 Saudrune data, allowing slight overshoot real corrected/aligned specimen can ), otherwise falls back data's combined range – e.g. specimen still raw, -yet-normalised digitization coordinates (pixels hundreds thousands), hard-coded c(0, 1) silently plot every point outside visible area. Pass numeric vector force specific limits instead, xlim/ylim (via ...) set x/y independently. ... arguments passed graphics::plot(); particular, xlim/ylim override axis_range independently x/y (see Details).

title: Plot a specimen following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — plot_fishmorph_points
text: Invisibly returns p x 2 matrix coordinates plotted, (individual matches one specimen) named list matrices, one per matching specimen.

title: Plot a specimen following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — plot_fishmorph_points
text: deliberately scheme-specific: requires least 21 landmarks following FISHMORPH protocol (errors otherwise), exchange much richer display generic scatterplot – colour-coded measurement segments legend, anatomical body outline/eye/reference lines, schematic scale bar, highlighting imputed, corrected, geometry-check-flagged landmarks. landmark scheme count (e.g. generic, n_landmarks-configurations simulate_fish_landmarks(), non-FISHMORPH digitization protocol), lighter-weight look FISHMORPH data without added detail, see plot_landmarks() instead. display choices fixed readability rather left graphics::plot()'s defaults: axes square (par(pty = "s")) share limits set axis_range (see ), xaxs/ yaxs = "" remove R's default 4% axis-range padding (together, two settings avoid wide band blank space R otherwise add whichever axis already match plotting device's width/height ratio), plot box drawn , fixed 20% beyond axis_range every side (e.g. [-0.2, 1.2] default [0, 1] convention) landmarks sitting right 0/1 edge – common FISHMORPH scheme's snout-tip, scale-bar caudal-fin landmarks – clipped border together halo number/arrow. Tick marks drawn short five evenly spaced positions within original, unpadded axis_range (quarter increments default [0, 1] range), tick labels always horizontal axes, axis titles read "X coordinates"/"Y coordinates". Landmark index numbers (labels = TRUE) placed landmark, whichever side points away rest configuration (landmarks 15 17, sit right main body axis, always placed ), connected back short arrow, drawn white halo behind bold number, stay legible never sit directly top fish outline coloured measurement segment. Landmarks 5, 13, 14 7 – upper part tightly clustered eye-socket group (5, 13, 7, 14, 6, 8) – additionally given explicit direction clear body outline (5 straight , 13 shallow -right, 14 steep -right – different angles, just different distances, two read sitting line cluster – 7 left), since generic rule alone still overlaps ; 6 nudged little right clears 8/15 ; 8 left entirely generic rule, like ventral landmarks (9, 11). Every offset computed physical inches (xinch()/yinch()) rather data units, layout looks regardless whether coordinates already normalised [0, 1] raw, -yet-corrected digitization pixels hundreds thousands. Landmarks 20 21 (scale bar) excluded numbering, plain scattered dots, entirely – see scale_unit .

title: Plot a specimen following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — plot_fishmorph_points
code: fish <- load_t26_saudrune_landmarks() plot_fishmorph_points(fish, specimen = 1)   # look a fish up by its code rather than by specimen/operator: the raw # operator-level data has two rows (one per operator) per fish, so both # digitizations are plotted side by side for comparison fish_ops <- load_t26_saudrune_landmarks("operators") one_code <- fish_ops$metadata$individual[1] plot_fishmorph_points(fish_ops, individual = one_code)   # if some specimens appear upside down or mirrored left-right, fix the # underlying coordinates (not just the display) for every specimen at # once, using landmarks that are always present and in the same role: fish_oriented <- standardize_orientation(fish) #> standardize_orientation(): 557 of 558 specimen(s) mirrored (165 horizontally, 555 vertically) to a consistent head-left, belly-down orientation. plot_fishmorph_points(fish_oriented, specimen = 1)   # disable the body outline / eye / reference lines, keeping only the # 11 coloured measurement segments (as in earlier package versions): plot_fishmorph_points(fish, specimen = 1, outline = FALSE)   # points estimated by impute_landmarks() are highlighted in red: # \donttest{ fish_imputed <- impute_landmarks(fish) #> Warning: 3 specimen(s) have a missing scale bar landmark (20 or 21); these cannot be estimated from shape covariation (they are not homologous shape landmarks) and are left as NA -- see fishmorph_segments()'s "zero-length or missing scale bar" warning. #> impute_landmarks(): estimated 260 missing anatomical landmark coordinate(s) using method = "tps". plot_fishmorph_points(fish_imputed, specimen = 1)  # }  # points fixed by correct_landmarks() are highlighted in blue: fish_fixed <- correct_landmarks(   fish, specimen = "T-26-0010_Operator_1",   points = c(9, 8, 11, 4), correct = 11, axis = "y" ) #> correct_landmarks(): specimen 'T-26-0010_Operator_1', landmark(s) 11: y set to 1664.167 (median of point(s) 4, 8, 9). plot_fishmorph_points(fish_fixed, specimen = "T-26-0010_Operator_1")   # landmarks implicated in a failed check_geometry() convention are # highlighted in orange: geom_check <- correct_landmarks(fish, rule = "check_geometry") plot_fishmorph_points(fish, specimen = 1, geometry_check = geom_check)   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Overlay the original photograph (requires the "jpeg" package and a # photograph in the same pixel coordinate system as the landmarks): plot_fishmorph_points(fish, specimen = 1, background_image = "specimen1.jpg") } # }

title: Overlay the body shape of every specimen in a species or a set of individuals — plot_fishmorph_shapes

title: Overlay the body shape of every specimen in a species or a set of individuals — plot_fishmorph_shapes
code: plot_fishmorph_shapes(   landmarks,   species = NULL,   individuals = NULL,   align = TRUE,   color = "steelblue4",   alpha = 0.15,   ... )

title: Overlay the body shape of every specimen in a species or a set of individuals — plot_fishmorph_shapes
text: landmarks object class "intrait_landmarks" least 21 two-dimensional landmarks digitized following FISHMORPH scheme (see fishmorph_segments()). species Character scalar, single value landmarks$metadata$species – every specimen species plotted. Exactly one species/individuals must supplied. individuals Character vector individual/specimen identifiers plot. Matched landmarks$metadata$individual column exists (fish's code selects every digitization , e.g. two rows load_t26_saudrune_landmarks("operators"), one per operator), otherwise directly specimen identifiers (dimnames). Exactly one species/individuals must supplied. align Logical, centre specimen's configuration centroid rescale unit centroid size overlaying (translation isotropic scale – rotation; see Details). Defaults TRUE. Set FALSE landmarks already fully comparable across specimens (e.g. Procrustes-aligned output gpa_fish()); overlaying raw digitized coordinates without aligning first mix genuine shape differences differences specimen's position/scale within photograph, informative shape. color Colour used every specimen's points outline. Defaults "steelblue4". alpha Transparency (0-1) applied color, overlapping specimens read denser cloud rather solid mass. Defaults 0.15. ... arguments passed graphics::plot().

title: Overlay the body shape of every specimen in a species or a set of individuals — plot_fishmorph_shapes
text: Invisibly returns named list (aligned, align = TRUE) p x 2 coordinate matrices actually plotted, one per matched specimen.

title: Overlay the body shape of every specimen in a species or a set of individuals — plot_fishmorph_shapes
text: specimen's landmark points FISHMORPH body outline (points 1-5-3-16-18-19-17-4-6, closed back 1 – path drawn plot_fishmorph_points()) drawn; 11 coloured measurement segments, eye, internal reference lines, landmark numbers, scale bar plot_fishmorph_points() omitted, since goal fast visual read shape variability across many specimens rather detailed inspection one . landmark missing (NA) given specimen silently dropped specimen's outline path (plot_fishmorph_points()), specimen's outline simply drawn fewer two outline landmarks present (points, , still shown). align = TRUE (default), specimen independently centred centroid divided centroid size (root sum squared landmark distances centroid; Bookstein, 1991; Dryden & Mardia, 2016) drawn – translation scale steps Procrustes superimposition, without rotation step. Rotation deliberately applied : unlike gpa_fish(), estimates single rotation jointly minimises squared point--point distance across whole sample – well-defined operation full data set jointly analysed, handful specimens picked afterwards display – function joint alignment fall back arbitrary species/individuals subset. practice means genuine differences fish happened oriented digitization photograph (tilt, mirroring) still show apparent shape differences; run standardize_orientation() landmarks beforehand remove source noise concern data set hand, pass already Procrustes-aligned coordinates align = FALSE.

title: Overlay the body shape of every specimen in a species or a set of individuals — plot_fishmorph_shapes
text: Bookstein FL (1991). Morphometric Tools Landmark Data: Geometry Biology. Cambridge University Press. Dryden IL, Mardia KV (2016). Statistical Shape Analysis, Applications R (2nd ed). Wiley.

text: landmarks object class "intrait_landmarks" "intrait_gpa", raw p x k x n array. Must two-dimensional. specimen Integer index character specimen identifier configuration plot. Defaults 1. labels Logical, label landmarks index. Defaults TRUE. background_image Optional path .jpg/.jpeg .png photograph specimen, drawn background layer beneath landmarks (e.g. visually check whether landmark placed body outline). meaningful raw, un-aligned digitized coordinates (warning issued landmarks "intrait_gpa" object, since Procrustes-aligned coordinates line original photograph). Requires (Suggested) jpeg package .jpg/.jpeg files, png .png files. Defaults NULL (background). flip_y Logical, flip background_image vertically plotting. Image files conventionally stored row 1 top, digitized landmark coordinates (read read_tps()/geomorph::digitize2d()) place origin bottom-left; default TRUE flips image lines landmarks without needing pre-flip file . Ignored background_image NULL. ... arguments passed graphics::plot().

title: Plot a single landmark configuration — plot_landmarks

title: Plot a single landmark configuration — plot_landmarks
text: deliberately generic viewer: makes assumption landmark count anatomical scheme, works equally well raw, Procrustes-aligned ("intrait_gpa"), simulated configurations (e.g. n_landmarks-shapes simulate_fish_landmarks()) – natural companion package's scheme-agnostic functions (gpa_fish(), detect_outliers(), correct_allometry(), intraspecific_variability(), morpho_space()). data digitized following FISHMORPH scheme specifically (Brosse et al. 2021, least 21 points), plot_fishmorph_points() usually informative – colours 11 measurement segments, draws body outline/eye/scale bar, highlights imputed/corrected/geometry- flagged landmarks – requires scheme errors anything fewer 21 landmarks; use function instead landmark configuration, lighter-weight look FISHMORPH data without added detail.

what: Import landmark coordinates from a generic long-format CSV file — read_landmarks_csv
title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic long-format CSV file — read_landmarks_csv
text: Reads landmark coordinates stored "long" (tidy) CSV file, one row per specimen/landmark combination, reshapes geomorph-style p x k x n array. Useful landmarks digitized outside tpsDig (e.g. ImageJ/Fiji, exported database), three-dimensional landmark configurations.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic long-format CSV file — read_landmarks_csv
code: read_landmarks_csv(   file,   specimen = "specimen",   landmark = "landmark",   coords = c("X", "Y"),   metadata = NULL,   ... )

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic long-format CSV file — read_landmarks_csv
text: file Character. Path CSV file, data.frame already loaded R. specimen Character. Name column identifying specimens. Defaults "specimen". landmark Character. Name column identifying landmarks (used order coordinates consistently within specimen). Defaults "landmark". coords Character vector column names holding coordinate values, order (e.g. c("X", "Y") 2D c("X", "Y", "Z") 3D). Defaults c("X", "Y"). metadata Optional data.frame specimen-level metadata, read_tps(). ... Additional arguments passed utils::read.csv() file path (ignored file already data.frame).

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic long-format CSV file — read_landmarks_csv
text: object class "intrait_landmarks" (see read_tps() details); scale set NULL since long-format CSV files carry digitization scale.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic long-format CSV file — read_landmarks_csv
code: df <- data.frame(   specimen = rep(c("fish_01", "fish_02"), each = 3),   landmark = rep(1:3, times = 2),   X = c(10, 15, 20, 11, 16, 21),   Y = c(20, 25, 20, 21, 26, 21) ) lm <- read_landmarks_csv(df) dim(lm$coords) #> [1] 3 2 2

what: Import landmark coordinates from a generic
title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic
text: Reads landmark coordinates stored "wide" spreadsheet layout, one row per specimen (per replicate digitization) one pair X/Y columns per landmark (e.g. X_1, Y_1, X_2, Y_2, ..., 1_X, 1_Y, 2_X, 2_Y, ...), reshapes geomorph-style p x k x n array. layout produced directly manual digitization spreadsheets (one column per landmark coordinate, filled hand copy-pasted image analysis software), opposed "long" (tidy) layout expected read_landmarks_csv(). optional second spreadsheet specimen-level identifications/metadata (e.g. taxonomic identification, capture date) can joined directly via species_file.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic
code: read_landmarks_xlsx(   file,   sheet = 1,   n_landmarks,   x_pattern = "X_{i}",   y_pattern = "Y_{i}",   id_cols = "Code",   specimen = NULL,   species_file = NULL,   species_sheet = 1,   species_by = NULL,   metadata = NULL,   ... )

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic
text: file Character. Path .xlsx/.xls file. sheet Sheet read: name 1-based index, passed readxl::read_excel(). Defaults first sheet. n_landmarks Integer, number landmarks digitized per specimen (.e. number X/Y column pairs look ). x_pattern, y_pattern Character, template X/Y column names landmark , "{}" placeholder landmark number. Defaults "X_{}"/"Y_{}" (e.g. X_1, X_2, ..., Y_1, Y_2, ..., layout single-digitization sheet); use "{}_X"/"{}_Y" sheet organised way round (e.g. 1_X, 1_Y, 2_X, 2_Y, ..., common replicate-digitization/repeatability sheets). id_cols Character vector one column names together identify row (e.g. specimen code, , sheet records one digitization per specimen, operator replicate/measurement column). Kept -metadata. Rows missing value id_cols[1] (typically blank spreadsheet row) dropped message. specimen NULL (default), single existing column name use directly specimen identifier. NULL, specimen identifiers built pasting together every column id_cols "_" (e.g. code_operator, code_replicate), almost always desired behaviour sheet records one digitization per specimen (row must resolve unique identifier). Duplicated identifiers made unique (warning) via make.unique(). species_file, species_sheet Optional path (sheet, sheet ) second spreadsheet one-row-per-specimen metadata (e.g. species identification) left-join onto landmark data. species_by Character, column name shared file species_file join . Defaults id_cols[1] (typically specimen code column). Every column species_file species_by added metadata -(renaming, attempt resolve conflicting/uncertain identifications – see Details). metadata Optional data.frame specimen-level metadata, merged top id_cols/species_file, read_tps(). ... Additional arguments passed readxl::read_excel() (e.g. skip, na, col_types).

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic
text: object class "intrait_landmarks" (see read_tps() details); scale set NULL since wide landmark sheet , , carry digitization scale (see fishmorph_segments()'s scale_cm argument landmarks 20-21 encode calibration segment, FISHMORPH scheme).

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic
text: function generalises private, one-cleaning script originally written import real T-26 Saudrune field data (Code/Utilisateur columns, X_1..X_21/Y_1..Y_21; see data-raw/t26_saudrune_prepare.R load_t26_saudrune_landmarks()) "wide spreadsheet" import can reused directly new field seasons surveys, without hand-written reshaping code, whatever specific column-naming convention (x_pattern/y_pattern) number landmarks (n_landmarks) involved. Coordinate cells coerced .numeric(); spreadsheet cells blank contain literal text "NA" become NA silently (normal, expected case genuinely missing digitizations), non-numeric cell content triggers warning naming offending column(s), since usually indicates data-entry problem (stray comment, mistyped value) rather intentionally missing landmark. species_file performs plain left-join (via match(), file's row order length always preserved even species_file duplicated missing keys) attempt reproduce project-specific identification-resolution logic (e.g. falling back preliminary/AI-assisted call curated one, flagging uncertain identifications) – identification sheet needs kind resolution use, R first (e.g. dplyr::coalesce() custom data.frame pass species_file/metadata), inspect FISHMORPH T-26 case study data-raw/t26_saudrune_prepare.R worked, non-generic example exactly kind resolution. Likewise, species_by values matched exactly (trimws()): inconsistent codes across two files (extra whitespace, stray "_" vs "-", parenthetical annotation) matched, normalised file/species_file beforehand needed.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a generic
code: if (requireNamespace("readxl", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("writexl", quietly = TRUE)) {   wide <- data.frame(     Code = c("fish_01", "fish_02", "fish_03"),     Utilisateur = c("Op1", "Op1", "Op2"),     X_1 = c(10, 11, 9), Y_1 = c(20, 21, 19),     X_2 = c(15, 16, 14), Y_2 = c(25, 26, 24),     X_3 = c(20, 21, 19), Y_3 = c(20, 21, 19)   )   ident <- data.frame(     Code = c("fish_01", "fish_02", "fish_03"),     species = c("Gobio occitaniae", "Gobio occitaniae", "Squalius cephalus")   )   xlsx_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")   ident_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")   writexl::write_xlsx(wide, xlsx_path)   writexl::write_xlsx(ident, ident_path)    lm <- read_landmarks_xlsx(     xlsx_path, n_landmarks = 3, id_cols = c("Code", "Utilisateur"),     species_file = ident_path, species_by = "Code"   )   dim(lm$coords)   lm$metadata } #>                Code Utilisateur           species #> fish_01_Op1 fish_01         Op1  Gobio occitaniae #> fish_02_Op1 fish_02         Op1  Gobio occitaniae #> fish_03_Op2 fish_03         Op2 Squalius cephalus

what: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
title: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
text: Reads tpsDig/tpsUtil-formatted .tps file containing two-dimensional landmark coordinates set specimens, returns geomorph-style array, optionally merged specimen-level metadata (species, population, standard length, etc.) together digitization scale (units per pixel) present file.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
code: read_tps(   file,   specID = c("imageID", "ID", "None"),   metadata = NULL,   negNA = FALSE )

title: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
text: file Character. Path .tps file. specID Character, one "imageID" (default), "ID", "None". Controls specimen identifiers built: IMAGE= field, ID= field, sequential specimen_1, specimen_2, ... labels. metadata Optional data.frame specimen-level metadata. Row names, column named specimen, must match specimen identifiers described specID. negNA Logical, defaults FALSE. TRUE, negative coordinates (commonly used flag missing landmarks tpsDig) converted NA.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
text: object class "intrait_landmarks", list elements: coords p x k x n numeric array raw (un-aligned) landmark coordinates, p landmarks k (2) dimensions n specimens, following geomorph array convention. scale named numeric vector scale factors (real-world units per pixel), one per specimen, taken SCALE= field TPS file (NA absent). metadata merged specimen metadata data.frame, NULL metadata supplied.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
text: TPS files de facto exchange format digitized landmark data geometric morphometrics (Rohlf, 2015). specimens file must share number landmarks (LM=); read_tps() throws informative error otherwise, since common landmark configuration required downstream Procrustes analysis. Three-dimensional (LM3=) TPS files currently supported; use read_landmarks_csv() 3D data.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
text: Rohlf FJ (2015). tps series software. Hystrix, 26(1), 9-12.

title: Import landmark coordinates from a tpsDig (.tps) file — read_tps
code: tps_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".tps") writeLines(c(   "LM=3", "10.0 20.0", "15.0 25.0", "20.0 20.0",   "IMAGE=fish_01.jpg", "ID=1", "SCALE=0.05",   "LM=3", "11.0 21.0", "16.0 26.0", "21.0 21.0",   "IMAGE=fish_02.jpg", "ID=2", "SCALE=0.05" ), tps_path) lm <- read_tps(tps_path) dim(lm$coords) #> [1] 3 2 2 lm$scale #> fish_01.jpg fish_02.jpg  #>        0.05        0.05

what: Reset the session-level group/species colour cache — reset_group_colors
title: Reset the session-level group/species colour cache — reset_group_colors
text: plot.intrait_morphospace() plot.intrait_traitspace() assign group colours small cache persists duration R session (see Details), species always drawn colour across separate plot calls – e.g. morphological space trait space built dataset, even though typically retains slightly different subset specimens (possibly species) upstream missing-data outlier filtering. reset_group_colors() clears cache, next plot call starts reassigning colours scratch, order species encountered.

title: Reset the session-level group/species colour cache — reset_group_colors
code: reset_group_colors()

title: Reset the session-level group/species colour cache — reset_group_colors
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Reset the session-level group/species colour cache — reset_group_colors
text: Call function starting work unrelated dataset R session (otherwise species assigned colours continuing wherever previous dataset's species left , harmless particularly meaningful), top script whose figures must depend happened run earlier session (full reproducibility colour assignment regardless call history).

title: Reset the session-level group/species colour cache — reset_group_colors
code: reset_group_colors()

what: Simulate a fish landmark data set — simulate_fish_landmarks
title: Simulate a fish landmark data set — simulate_fish_landmarks
text: Generates simulated two-dimensional landmark data set representing several fish "species" distinct mean body shapes sizes, several individuals per species drawn population, (optionally) several digitization replicates per individual. used throughout package documentation vignette demonstrate full intraitR workflow without requiring real digitized data, can used starting point (set.seed()) teaching testing analysis code applying real specimens.

title: Simulate a fish landmark data set — simulate_fish_landmarks
code: simulate_fish_landmarks(   n_per_species = 20,   species = c("Species_A", "Species_B", "Species_C"),   n_landmarks = 12,   n_replicates = 3,   seed = 123 )

title: Simulate a fish landmark data set — simulate_fish_landmarks
text: n_per_species Integer, number individuals simulated per species. Defaults 20. species Character vector species labels. Defaults c("Species_A", "Species_B", "Species_C"). n_landmarks Integer, number landmarks per configuration (placed evenly spaced angles around fish-body-shaped ellipse). Defaults 12. n_replicates Integer, number digitization replicates simulated per individual (1 replication). Defaults 3. seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. Defaults 123.

title: Simulate a fish landmark data set — simulate_fish_landmarks
text: object class "intrait_landmarks" (see read_tps()), metadata columns specimen, species, population, standard_length_mm replicate. scale set 1 every specimen (coordinates already expressed simulated millimetres).

title: Simulate a fish landmark data set — simulate_fish_landmarks
text: Shape simulated isotropic Gaussian noise added per-species mean ellipse, plus independent per-individual per-digitization-replicate noise components; centroid size ( proxy standard length) drawn normal distribution. per-replicate noise deliberately much smaller per-individual per-species variance components, simulated data set well suited illustrate intraspecific_variability() (genuine among-individual among-species variation) measurement_error() (small, quantifiable digitization noise).

title: Simulate a fish landmark data set — simulate_fish_landmarks
code: fish <- simulate_fish_landmarks(n_per_species = 5, n_replicates = 2) fish #> <intrait_landmarks> #>   30 specimens, 12 landmarks, 2 dimensions #>   Scale available for 30/30 specimens #>   Metadata columns: specimen, species, population, standard_length_mm, replicate  head(fish$metadata) #>                                  specimen   species population #> Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A      Pop_2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A      Pop_2 #> Species_A_ind03_rep1 Species_A_ind03_rep1 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind03_rep2 Species_A_ind03_rep2 Species_A      Pop_1 #>                      standard_length_mm replicate #> Species_A_ind01_rep1               74.4         1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2               74.4         2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1               72.9         1 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2               72.9         2 #> Species_A_ind03_rep1               65.4         1 #> Species_A_ind03_rep2               65.4         2

what: Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — simulate_fishmorph_points
title: Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — simulate_fishmorph_points
text: Generates simulated data set 21 (22) landmarks per specimen, positioned following Brosse et al. (2021) FISHMORPH digitization scheme (see fishmorph_segments()), several simulated "species" distinct body proportions. removes dependency real digitized pictures examples, teaching, testing fishmorph_segments(), fishmorph_ratios(), trait_space(), plot_fishmorph_points().

title: Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — simulate_fishmorph_points
code: simulate_fishmorph_points(   n_per_species = 15,   species = c("Species_A", "Species_B", "Species_C"),   n_replicates = 1,   curvature = FALSE,   scale_cm = 1,   px_per_cm = 12,   seed = 123 )

title: Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — simulate_fishmorph_points
text: n_per_species Integer, number individuals simulated per species. Defaults 15. species Character vector species labels. Defaults c("Species_A", "Species_B", "Species_C"), simulated increasingly elongate--deep body proportions. n_replicates Integer, number digitization replicates simulated per individual (1 replication). Defaults 1. curvature Logical, also simulate 22nd landmark body curvature correction standard length (see fishmorph_segments()). Defaults FALSE (21 landmarks , straight-line body length). scale_cm Numeric, real-world distance centimetres represented simulated scale bar (landmarks 20-21). Defaults 1. px_per_cm Numeric, digitization units per centimetre used build simulated scale bar body proportions (arbitrary; ratio body landmark coordinates matters). Defaults 12. seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. Defaults 123.

title: Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — simulate_fishmorph_points
text: object class "intrait_landmarks" (see read_tps()), 21 (22) landmarks per specimen fixed order described fishmorph_segments(), metadata columns specimen, individual, species, population, replicate. scale set NULL (scale bar embedded landmarks 20-21, per FISHMORPH scheme, rather carried external metadata).

title: Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — simulate_fishmorph_points
text: landmark scheme mixes true body-shape landmarks (1-19) external scale-bar reference (20-21) , optionally, non-homologous curvature-correction point (22); intended use fishmorph_segments(), Generalised Procrustes Analysis (gpa_fish()), assumes landmarks homologous shape coordinates.

title: Simulate landmark data following the FISHMORPH point digitization scheme — simulate_fishmorph_points
code: fish <- simulate_fishmorph_points(n_per_species = 5, n_replicates = 2) fish #> <intrait_landmarks> #>   30 specimens, 21 landmarks, 2 dimensions #>   Metadata columns: specimen, individual, species, population, replicate  head(fish$metadata) #>                                  specimen      individual   species population #> Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A_ind01_rep1 Species_A_ind01 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A_ind01_rep2 Species_A_ind01 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A_ind02_rep1 Species_A_ind02 Species_A      Pop_2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A_ind02_rep2 Species_A_ind02 Species_A      Pop_2 #> Species_A_ind03_rep1 Species_A_ind03_rep1 Species_A_ind03 Species_A      Pop_1 #> Species_A_ind03_rep2 Species_A_ind03_rep2 Species_A_ind03 Species_A      Pop_1 #>                      replicate #> Species_A_ind01_rep1         1 #> Species_A_ind01_rep2         2 #> Species_A_ind02_rep1         1 #> Species_A_ind02_rep2         2 #> Species_A_ind03_rep1         1 #> Species_A_ind03_rep2         2

what: Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates — species_sensitivity
title: Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates — species_sensitivity
text: species, quantifies much replacing species' centroid one real individuals changes estimated functional richness PCA space, every species stays fixed centroid, following species-level sensitivity index Bertrand (2026, Section "Species-level sensitivity index"). complements bootstrap_functional_space()'s community-wide comparison asking finer-grained question: species drive difference individual-based centroid-based functional richness, individual effects consistent highly variable?

title: Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates — species_sensitivity
code: species_sensitivity(   x,   groups = NULL,   method = c("convexhull", "dendrogram", "tpd", "hypervolume"),   n_axes = NULL,   var_threshold = 0.98,   log_transform = TRUE,   scale = TRUE,   dendrogram_linkage = "average",   tpd_alpha = 0.95,   tpd_bw_factor = 0.5,   tpd_n_divisions = NULL,   hv_bw_method = "silverman",   hv_samples_per_point = 500 )

title: Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates — species_sensitivity
text: x Either object class "intrait_traitspace" (trait_space(), built groups supplied), data.frame/matrix numeric traits (one row per individual), case groups must also supplied log_transform/scale preprocessing trait_space() applied PCA described . groups Required x raw trait table (one value per individual, typically species identity); ignored (taken x$groups) x "intrait_traitspace" object. method, n_axes, var_threshold, log_transform, scale, dendrogram_linkage, tpd_alpha, tpd_bw_factor, tpd_n_divisions, hv_bw_method, hv_samples_per_point bootstrap_functional_space(): method selects functional-richness measure recomputed reference configuration every single-individual replacement ("convexhull", default, n-dimensional convex-hull volume; "dendrogram", total branch length UPGMA functional dendrogram; "tpd", Trait Probability Density functional richness; "hypervolume", Gaussian-kernel hypervolume), n_axes PCA axes used (auto-selected via var_threshold NULL), computed fresh PCA x$X (freshly standardised x). , method = "convexhull" strictly requires nlevels(groups) > n_axes; methods warn instead. "tpd"/"hypervolume", kernel bandwidth (, "tpd", evaluation grid) likewise computed full individual-level PCA scores reused, unchanged, fd_ref every one nrow(x) single-individual replacements, comparability reason explained .

title: Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates — species_sensitivity
text: object class "intrait_species_sensitivity", list elements: summary (data.frame, one row per species, columns species, n_individuals, mean_dFD, min_dFD, max_dFD – species-level index, .e. Bertrand (2026)'s mu_k range, original levels(groups) order), individual (long-format data.frame one row per individual, columns species dFD, full transparency beyond per-species summary), fd_ref (community-wide centroid-based reference richness), method, n_axes, var_explained. dedicated print() plot() methods.

title: Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates — species_sensitivity
text: focal species k individuals = 1, ..., n_k, centroid n_axes-dimensional PCA space replaced, one individual time, individual's PCA scores, every species remains centroid; functional richness modified n_species-point configuration FD_{k,}. replacement expressed percentage change relative (unmodified) centroid-based reference richness fd_ref: $$\Delta FD_{k,} (\%) = 100 \times (FD_{k,} - FD_{ref}) / FD_{ref}$$ positive dFD means individual, alone stood species' centroid, expand estimated functional space; negative dFD means contract . mean_dFD (mu_k) summarises average tendency species' individuals, min_dFD/max_dFD describe heterogeneity individual effects within species – wide range indicates unusual individuals rather consistent species-level tendency (see Bertrand, 2026, worked examples patterns real data). Unlike bootstrap_functional_space(), index requires resampling significance test: every replacement deterministic (one individual, one recomputed richness value), species_sensitivity() exact given x/groups/n_axes/method, simulation-based. Species one individual still receive (single-valued) mean_dFD, min_dFD == max_dFD useful "range" speak , expected, error. Every individual, across every species, requires functional-richness recomputation (one call per individual x/groups, per species), computationally demanding two functional-space functions large data set – Bertrand (2026)'s regional panel, instance, 1,302 individuals – especially method = "hypervolume" (far expensive four measures per call, see bootstrap_functional_space()). individual's replacement independent every 's, , exactly bootstrap_functional_space(), distributed automatically across future.apply's workers package installed parallel future::plan() set beforehand; plan set, without future.apply, runs sequentially identical results.

title: Species-level sensitivity index for functional space estimates — species_sensitivity
text: Bertrand P (2026). Intraspecific trait variability shapes functional space freshwater fish French Guiana assemblages. M2 Biodiversity Ecology Evolution (BEE) internship report, Lille University / Centre de Recherche sur la Biodiversite et l'Environnement (CRBE, AQUAECO team), unpublished, supervised . Toussaint S. Brosse. Villeger S, Mason NWH, Mouillot D (2008). New multidimensional functional diversity indices multifaceted framework functional ecology. Ecology, 89(8), 2290-2301. Petchey OL, Gaston KJ (2002). Functional diversity (FD), species richness community composition. Ecology Letters, 5(3), 402-411. Carmona CP, de Bello F, Mason NWH, Leps J (2019). Trait probability density (TPD): measuring functional diversity across scales based TPD R. Ecology, 100(12), e02876. Blonder B, Lamanna C, Violle C, Enquist BJ (2014). n-dimensional hypervolume. Global Ecology Biogeography, 23(5), 595-609. Blonder B, Morrow CB, Maitner B, Harris DJ, Lamanna C, Violle C, Enquist BJ, Kerkhoff AJ (2018). New approaches delineating n-dimensional hypervolumes. Methods Ecology Evolution, 9(2), 305-319.

what: Standardize every specimen to the same head-left, belly-down orientation — standardize_orientation
title: Standardize every specimen to the same head-left, belly-down orientation — standardize_orientation
text: Digitization sources vary picture happens oriented (fish facing left right, right-side upside ), whether Y increases upward (standard Cartesian convention) downward (image/pixel convention). Rather per-plot display toggle (earlier version plot_fishmorph_points()'s flip_y_points argument offered), standardize_orientation() checks , needed, mirrors every specimen's actual coordinates using two pairs landmarks present, anatomically two points, every FISHMORPH digitization: snout tip caudal fin base (1, 2) fix left-right orientation, top/bottom body deepest point (3, 4) fix dorsal-ventral orientation.

title: Standardize every specimen to the same head-left, belly-down orientation — standardize_orientation

code: # real T-26 Saudrune data; landmark indices follow the FISHMORPH scheme # (see ?fishmorph_segments) fish <- load_t26_saudrune_landmarks() distances <- list(SL = c(1, 2), BD = c(3, 4)) ratios <- morpho_ratios(fish, distances, norm_by = "SL") summary_traits(ratios[, "BD_ratio", drop = FALSE], fish$metadata$species) #>                        group    trait   n      mean          sd    min     max #> 1                            BD_ratio   2 0.2115000 0.001838478 0.2102  0.2128 #> 2        Barbatula barbatula BD_ratio  36 0.3396667 0.899758647 0.1628  5.5873 #> 3              Barbus barbus BD_ratio  10 0.2312400 0.005458775 0.2247  0.2411 #> 4           Gobio occitaniae BD_ratio 338 0.2890476 0.569884042 0.0163 10.7273 #> 5           Lepomis gibbosus BD_ratio   4 0.4071000 0.023965948 0.3858  0.4337 #> 6    Leuciscus burdigalensis BD_ratio  14 0.2593286 0.027835601 0.2161  0.3091 #> 7          Perca fluviatilis BD_ratio  16 0.2827000 0.010066777 0.2628  0.3001 #> 8          Phoxinus phoxinus BD_ratio  34 0.2373794 0.017063691 0.1959  0.2699 #> 9  Phoxinus phoxinus/bigerri BD_ratio   8 0.2441125 0.018669489 0.2246  0.2738 #> 10         Squalius cephalus BD_ratio  96 0.2537021 0.023546241 0.0934  0.2995

Pairwise differences dispersion groups tested null distribution obtained randomly permuting group labels.

title: Test differences in functional trait dispersion between groups — trait_disparity

title: Test differences in functional trait dispersion between groups — trait_disparity
text: permutation procedure reassigns n specimens groups random (preserving observed group sizes), recomputes group's trait variance, derives null distribution pairwise differences iter permutations plus observed assignment (standard (iter + 1)-permutation correction; Anderson, 2001). group significantly higher trait variance another occupies, average, larger region standardised functional trait space, consistent greater morphological ecological generalism within group. Groups fewer two specimens receive disparity NA excluded permutation test. function complements intraspecific_variability(), reports shape disparity (Procrustes coordinates) univariate coefficients variation, test group differences dispersion multivariate trait space. bootstrap_functional_space() species_sensitivity(), iter permutations independent one another distributed automatically across future.apply's workers (Suggested) package installed parallel future::plan() set beforehand; otherwise runs sequentially, identical results.

what: Build a functional trait space from a numeric trait table — trait_space
title: Build a functional trait space from a numeric trait table — trait_space
text: Constructs low-dimensional functional trait space table numeric traits (e.g. FISHMORPH ecomorphological ratios produced fishmorph_ratios(), linear traits/ratios morpho_ratios()), Principal Component Analysis metric multidimensional scaling (Principal Coordinate Analysis) Euclidean distance matrix, two standard approaches used build functional trait spaces comparative ecology (e.g. Villéger et al., 2017).

title: Build a functional trait space from a numeric trait table — trait_space
code: trait_space(   traits,   groups = NULL,   method = c("pca", "pcoa"),   log_transform = TRUE,   scale = TRUE,   axes = c(1, 2),   na_action = c("fail", "omit", "impute_mean", "impute_group_mean", "missforest",     "missforest_phylo"),   missforest_ntree = 100,   missforest_maxiter = 10,   tree = NULL,   missforest_phylo_k = 10,   flag_outliers = TRUE,   outlier_threshold = 3,   outlier_min_n = 5,   remove_outliers = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'intrait_traitspace' print(x, ...)

title: Build a functional trait space from a numeric trait table — trait_space
text: traits data.frame matrix numeric traits, one row per specimen species. Non-numeric columns dropped warning (included ordination, grouping still auto-detected species column present; see groups). Constant (zero-variance) numeric columns also dropped warning, since carry information ordination rescaled unit variance; commonly happens incidental numeric metadata (e.g. digitization replicate counter) carried fishmorph_segments()/fishmorph_ratios() passed trait_space() unfiltered. non-finite value (Inf/ -Inf, ratio zero-length denominator segment) always error regardless na_action (treated ordinary missing value – see Details). groups Optional factor (character vector), one value per row traits, used colour/group observations plotting. NULL traits contains species column, used automatically. method Character, one "pca" (default, Principal Component Analysis via stats::prcomp()) "pcoa" (Principal Coordinate Analysis / classical multidimensional scaling, via stats::cmdscale(), Euclidean distance matrix , optionally transformed standardised, trait data — equivalent PCA arbitrary rotation sign). log_transform Logical, apply log10(x + 1) transformation every numeric trait centring/scaling ordination. Defaults TRUE. Ratio traits (e.g. fishmorph_ratios() morpho_ratios()) bounded zero often right-skewed, log(x + 1) transformation (+ 1 accommodating traits can legitimately equal zero, e.g. Villéger et al., 2010, exception rules) common practice ordination. Requires trait values non-negative; set FALSE skip (e.g. traits can negative, PCA scores fed back second ordination). scale Logical, standardise (centre scale unit variance) traits building trait space, optional log transformation (recommended traits different scales different variances). Defaults TRUE. axes Integer vector length 2, ordination axes retain plotting. Defaults c(1, 2). na_action Character, handle missing values numeric trait columns: "fail" (default) stops error, previous versions; "omit" removes affected rows (specimens/species) reports many dropped; "impute_mean" replaces missing values corresponding column mean; "impute_group_mean" replaces missing values mean trait within group (groups, auto-detected species column), falling back column mean, warning, group entirely missing trait; "missforest" uses random-forest-based iterative imputation (missForest::missForest(), Stekhoven & Bühlmann, 2012) numeric trait matrix, using groups (available) additional predictor; "missforest_phylo" also augments predictor matrix phylogenetic PCoA axes (see phylo_pcoa(), tree/missforest_phylo_k) species groups, phylogenetically related species can inform 's imputed values addition shared species identity – falling back plain "missforest", warning explaining , phylogenetic axes used (groups, fewer 3 species matched tree, "ape" installed, etc.). "omit" every imputation option print message() reporting number rows removed values imputed (plus, "missforest"/ "missforest_phylo", --bag normalised RMSE imputation, , "missforest_phylo", many phylogenetic axes matched species actually used), never silent operation. missforest_ntree, missforest_maxiter Number trees per forest maximum number iterations passed missForest::missForest() na_action "missforest"/ "missforest_phylo"; ignored otherwise. Default missForest's defaults (100 10). tree Used na_action = "missforest_phylo": object class "phylo" (e.g. ape::read.tree()), NULL (default) use bundled load_fishmorph_phylogeny() tree. missforest_phylo_k Used na_action = "missforest_phylo": maximum number phylogenetic PCoA axes add predictors. Defaults 10. flag_outliers Logical, screen potential within-group (e.g. within-species) outliers – specimens unusually far members group standardised trait space – report (see Details outlier_threshold/outlier_min_n). Requires groups (auto-detected species column); effect ($outlier_screen element, message) grouping available, since "distance individuals species" undefined without species labels. Defaults TRUE. never removes observation: flags candidates visual/manual review (e.g. plot_landmarks() plot_fishmorph_points()) deciding whether exclusion warranted. outlier_threshold Numeric, number median absolute deviations (MAD) group's median within-group distance beyond specimen flagged; convention detect_outliers()'s threshold. Defaults 3. outlier_min_n Integer, minimum number specimens group must outlier flagging attempted ; groups smaller still get computed distance ($outlier_screen) never flagged (NA), since median/MAD computed points reliable reference. Defaults 5. remove_outliers Logical, actually exclude every specimen flagged flag_outliers trait matrix building ordination (rather flagging review, default). Requires flag_outliers = TRUE (error raised otherwise, since nothing remove). Defaults FALSE: removing specimens changes ordination statistic derived (e.g. trait_disparity(), bootstrap_functional_space()), opt-rather automatic, every removal still recorded $removed_outliers (see Return) transparency reproducibility – always confirm flagged specimens genuinely reflect error (e.g. via plot_landmarks()/plot_fishmorph_points()) turning given data set, rather treating default cleaning step. x object class "intrait_traitspace", returned trait_space(). ... Currently unused.

Pooling across species, naive global outlier screen , mostly flags genuine interspecific morphological diversity rather digitization identification errors (see worked pooled-vs-within- species comparison demo(pipeline_T26_saudrune)); computing automatically, per group, inside trait_space() removes need subset hand. single extreme specimen otherwise tight species can also visibly distort ordination every group, inflating axis ranges/variance explained: species-level outlier therefore often right first thing check functional space "look right" (widely spread groups collapsed one corner plot), considering, e.g., different na_action transformation. detect_outliers(), flags candidates – never removes anything automatically, since ad hoc, undocumented removal "bad-looking" specimens threat reproducibility; always inspect flagged specimen (e.g. plot_landmarks()/plot_fishmorph_points(), original photograph available) deciding whether exclude , record decision (e.g. QC log, data-raw/t26_saudrune_prepare.R bundled real data set) rather silently dropping rows. Euclidean, Mahalanobis, distance (like detect_outliers()), account correlations among traits; intended fast, transparent first pass, definitive statistical test. Setting remove_outliers = TRUE goes one step actually excludes every flagged specimen ordination built (rather flagging ): X, groups, scores returned object describe cleaned data set, $removed_outliers records exactly specimens dropped , exclusion remains fully reproducible auditable (e.g. reportable manuscript's methods) rather undocumented, ad hoc edit made calling trait_space(). deliberately opt-(FALSE default): removing data always changes downstream results conscious, visually-confirmed decision (see ), something happens silently just threshold crossed.
