title: intraitR 1.1.0
text: fishmorph_ratios() gains landmarks argument: "intrait_landmarks" object/array originally passed fishmorph_segments(). missing zero-length scale bar (landmarks 20-21) makes every one fishmorph_segments()’s 11 measurements NA specimen, since pixel--centimetre conversion factor single per-specimen scalar applied – previously also made nine ratios NA specimen, even every anatomical landmark (1-19) perfectly digitized. ratio always (segment / segment) computed within specimen, unknown/missing scale factor cancels division exactly, landmarks lets nine ratios (– absolute segments, MBl) recomputed directly raw pixel-space landmark distances instead. applied specimens whose segments row entirely NA (missing-scale-bar signature); specimen just one missing anatomical landmark, single geometry_check-flagged segment, left untouched, since mixing pixel-space calibrated values specimen defeat quality control. New internal helper .fishmorph_pixel_segments() (R/utils-internal.R) extracts shared pixel-distance geometry engine fishmorph_segments() (pure refactor, behaviour change ) functions compute 11 raw measurements identically. Fixed bug expect_equal() reference value two impute_landmarks() regression tests (method = "impute_mean" "impute_group_mean") computed column/group mean including point deleted imputed, rather excluding impute_landmarks() (mean(x, na.rm = TRUE) computed value set NA). test-defect, caught real devtools::test() run; impute_landmarks()’s imputation logic unaffected. Fixed bug trait_space() crash cryptic Error (stopCriterion(...)) : missing value TRUE/FALSE needed na_action = "missforest" (otherwise silently corrupt ordination na_action) trait column contained non-finite value (Inf/-Inf), typically ratio zero-length denominator segment (e.g. degenerate duplicated landmark; see fishmorph_segments()/fishmorph_ratios()). Inf/-Inf detected .na()/anyNA(), value used pass straight every na_action unimputed; missForest::missForest()’s internal convergence check computed Inf - Inf = NaN, crashing () loop. trait_space() now checks non-finite trait values unconditionally, missing-value handling, stops informative error naming offending column(s) row(s), regardless na_action. bootstrap_functional_space() gains composition argument: communities x species matrix (presence/absence abundance; presence used) giving species composition one communities/sites. supplied, centroid-based-reference-vs- bootstrap-distribution principle used whole species pool repeated independently community, restricted community’s species, using shared PCA space method-specific auxiliary quantities (kernel bandwidth/grid) results stay comparable across communities. Results returned new $communities data.frame (community, n_species, fd_obs, fd_expected, fd_sd, ses – Standardized Effect Size, (fd_obs - fd_expected) / fd_sd – p_value) new $community_boot list raw per-community bootstrap vectors, addition unchanged whole-pool fd_ref/fd_boot outputs. print() now reports per-community summary present, plot() gains type = c("pool", "communities") argument: "communities" draws dot (“forest”) plot ses per community, coloured significance – chosen per-community histogram grid (impractical handful communities) raw obs-vs-expected scatter (directly comparable across communities different species richness, unlike ses). Species-column matching defensive mismatch composition’s column names groups’s species labels: duplicated column names now error immediately (rather silently using first match), unmatched columns reported name, just count, make spelling/case/whitespace mismatch easy spot. Fixed bug genuine "" species label (e.g. unresolved/ unidentified specimen, can occur real field data) caused composition[, matched_sp] fail “subscript bounds” – [-indexing never matches ""-named column even one exists (see ?Extract); column selection now goes match() instead (fix already used group_colors()’s ""-label handling). plot(type = "communities") now always keeps SES = 0 (dashed reference line every point’s connecting segment) inside plotted x-range, even every community’s ses sits far 0 relative spread – previously x-axis limits computed range(ses), push 0 -screen silently truncate reference line/segments plot edge. impute_landmarks() gains three statistical imputation methods alongside existing geometric-morphometric "tps"/"regression": "impute_mean", "impute_group_mean", "missforest", mirroring trait_space()’s na_action options applied directly raw landmark coordinates rather derived traits. "impute_mean" replaces missing coordinate column mean across specimens; "impute_group_mean" uses within-group mean instead (new groups argument, auto-detected landmarks$metadata$species available; falls back overall mean, warning, group entirely missing coordinate); "missforest" uses missForest::missForest() across landmark coordinates jointly, groups optional auxiliary predictor (new missforest_ntree/ missforest_maxiter arguments). treat coordinate ordinary numeric variable ignore shape covariation, "tps"/"regression" remain preferable whenever enough complete specimens available; statistical options meant exploratory use complete configurations remain geomorph::estimate.missing() work reliably. New function phylo_pcoa(): derives phylogenetic ordination axes tree (ape::drop.tip() + ape::cophenetic.phylo() + ape::pcoa(), optional phytools::force.ultrametric() coercion Cailliez/Lingoes negative-eigenvalue correction), returning species + PCoA1..k data.frame deliberately shaped passed directly traits [trait_space()]. lets phylogenetic space built exact ordination/bootstrap machinery already used morphological trait spaces ([bootstrap_functional_space()], [species_sensitivity()]), functional phylogenetic diversity loss can compared using statistics. Deliberately scoped generic tree--axes step : taxonomic name resolution, tree sourcing, external trait/occurrence data assembly considered scope left user’s data-preparation code. Adds ape phytools Suggests. New na_action/method option, "missforest_phylo", added everywhere "missforest" already available – trait_space(), fishmorph_segments(), fishmorph_ratios(), impute_landmarks() – augmenting missForest::missForest()’s predictors phylogenetic PCoA axes ([phylo_pcoa()]) computed tree (user-supplied ape::phylo object, , default, package’s newly bundled load_fishmorph_phylogeny() tree), matched row’s groups (species) label. lets imputation borrow information phylogenetically related species, just correlations among traits/coordinates raw species factor, plain "missforest". Matching robust whether species labels use space, underscore, dot genus/species separator (new internal .canon_species_name() helper), since bundled tree’s tip labels use "Genus.species" convention. Phylogenetic augmentation designed never turn working "missforest" call hard error: groups/tree available, fewer 3 species can matched tree, ape package missing, "missforest_phylo" falls back plain "missforest" (phylogenetic predictors) explanatory warning() rather stopping. New shared arguments tree (default NULL, meaning “use bundled phylogeny”) missforest_phylo_k (default 10, number phylogenetic PCoA axes used auxiliary predictors) added alongside existing missforest_ntree/ missforest_maxiter. New function load_fishmorph_phylogeny() bundled data file inst/extdata/Phylogeny/FishMORPH_Phylogeny.rds: loads package’s default phylogenetic tree (ape::phylo object, 10,705 tips) used default tree "missforest_phylo" () available directly input phylo_pcoa(). Provenance note: exact source/citation tree independently verified package’s documentation; users relying publication confirm cite original source (e.g. phylogeny associated FISHMORPH trait database, Brosse et al. rather citing ?load_fishmorph_phylogeny. phylo_pcoa() bug fix: species-name matching species/tree normalised spaces underscores (gsub(" ", "_", ...)), silently failed match species tree using dot-separated tip labels ("Genus.species"), newly bundled load_fishmorph_phylogeny() tree . Matching now goes .canon_species_name() helper used "missforest_phylo" (), collapsing runs spaces, dots, underscores single underscore comparison; matched species consequently returned canonical underscore form. correct_geometry() bug fix: step 1’s isotropic rescale [0, 1] left intrait_landmarks object’s $scale element (real-world units per digitization pixel, see read_tps()) uncorrected, silently making linear_distances()/morpho_ratios() return wrong real-world distances rescaled coordinates afterwards (visual, -screen size every specimen intentionally equalized step, specimen’s true, individual real-world size must still recoverable downstream). $scale now divided per-specimen scale_factor applied coordinates, real-world distances computed correct_geometry() now identical; fishmorph_segments() never affected, since always re-derives pixel--real-world factor fresh scale bar’s current length rather trusting stored value. new message() reports many specimens’ $scale updated way. plot_fishmorph_points(): digitization scale bar (landmarks 20-21) now drawn solid, filled bar border (two triangle point markers, thin open line), placed lower near plot’s origin, caption placed directly rather . caption now built automatically "1 <unit> = <length>" (e.g. "1 cm = 3.2"), <length> specimen’s digitized scale-bar length, rather previous fixed "scale (1 cm)" text. scale_label argument replaced scale_unit (default "cm", matching FISHMORPH protocol’s standard 1 cm calibration segment), letting users specify real-world unit data set actually digitized (e.g. "mm", "dm", "m", label); set scale_unit = NULL omit caption entirely (bar still drawn). breaking change code calling plot_fishmorph_points(..., scale_label = ...). New plot_correlation_circle(): draws classical correlation circle (variable factor map) trait_space() ordination – trait arrow Pearson correlation two plotted axes, inside unit circle, optional inner circle radius sqrt(0.5) marking conventional “well represented” threshold. Unlike plot raw loadings, arrow length directly comparable across traits meaningful regardless method ("pca"/"pcoa") scale. Drawn without surrounding box: tick values run along y = 0/x = 0 reference lines origin, labelled axis name (e.g. "PC1"), small italic font, just outside circle centred reference line. bootstrap_functional_space() gains method argument functional-richness measure computed PCA-based trait space: "convexhull" (default, unchanged behaviour: n-dimensional convex-hull volume via geometry::convhulln()), "dendrogram" (total branch length UPGMA functional dendrogram, Petchey & Gaston 2002 – needs extra Suggested package), "tpd" (Trait Probability Density richness via TPD package, Carmona et al. 2019), "hypervolume" (Gaussian-kernel hypervolume via hypervolume package, Blonder et al. 2014, 2018). "tpd"/"hypervolume", kernel bandwidth (, "tpd", evaluation grid) computed full individual-level data reused, unchanged, centroid-based reference every bootstrap draw, richness values stay comparable across draws; new dendrogram_linkage, tpd_alpha, tpd_bw_factor, tpd_n_divisions, hv_bw_method, hv_samples_per_point arguments tune . TPD hypervolume new Suggested dependencies, required respective method used. print()/plot() methods "intrait_bootstrap_fspace" now report method used. plot.intrait_bootstrap_fspace() also drops previous FD_ref text annotation histogram favour marking fd_ref (red) bootstrap mean fd_boot_mean (blue) directly x-axis, matching dashed vertical line. species_sensitivity() gains method argument (matching dendrogram_linkage/tpd_*/hv_* tuning arguments) bootstrap_functional_space(), computing species-level sensitivity index "convexhull" (default), "dendrogram", "tpd", "hypervolume" functional richness instead convex-hull volume. print()/plot() methods report method used. New compare_functional_richness(): runs bootstrap_functional_space() per requested method ("convexhull", "dendrogram", "tpd", "hypervolume"; four default) data tabulates results side side (fd_ref, fd_boot_mean, pct_diff, p_value, significant), methodological triangulation across richness measures. method whose package missing, otherwise errors, recorded skipped row rather failing whole comparison. Optional seed argument pairs bootstrap draws across methods. dedicated print() (summary table + agreement count) plot() (dot--whisker comparison, one row per method) methods. Fixed group/species colours staying consistent plot.intrait_morphospace() plot.intrait_traitspace() built dataset: colours previously derived call’s nlevels(groups)/position within observed factor levels, species get different colour whenever two objects happened retain different subset species upstream missing-data outlier filtering. Colours now looked label session-persistent cache, given species always gets colour assigned, regardless species present later call. New reset_group_colors() clears cache (e.g. unrelated dataset, full reproducibility irrespective call history). New plot_fishmorph_shapes(): overlays landmark points body outline (FISHMORPH outline path used plot_fishmorph_points()) every specimen given species, explicit vector individuals, single figure – landmark numbers, measurement segments, eye, internal reference lines – fast visual read shape variability across many specimens . default (align = TRUE) specimen independently centred centroid rescaled unit centroid size (translation scale , rotation) drawn, overlay compares shape rather raw digitization position/size; set align = FALSE already-comparable coordinates (e.g. gpa_fish() output). Axis tick labels use grDevices::axisTicks() (“round numbers” computation behind R’s default axes) rather evenly spaced raw fractions data range, since range data-driven generally already round – unlike plot_fishmorph_points()’s fixed [0, 1] convention, whose quarter increments round construction. Fixed crash (“attempt use zero-length variable name”) plot.intrait_morphospace()/plot.intrait_traitspace() whenever group level empty string "" (e.g. unresolved species identification stored "" rather NA source data): session-level colour cache previously stored one colour per group assign()ing environment variable named raw label, errors "". Colours now stored single named vector instead, restriction; "" now treated like distinct label. Fixed follow-bug ""-label fix : colour cache’s lookup, cache[uniq], relied [’s character-name matching, R documents never matching "" index "" name even name genuinely present (?Extract: “Neither empty (’’) NA indices match names, even empty missing names.”). ""-labelled group therefore still assigned NA instead cached colour lookup, even though storage worked correctly. Lookup now uses match(), exception. New group_colors(): returns exact group/species colours plot.intrait_morphospace()/plot.intrait_traitspace() use (use), group/color data.frame, order legend – building single shared legend across several panels (e.g. par(mfrow = c(2, 2)), plotted legend = FALSE) without reimplementing guessing underlying colour assignment. Accepts either object $groups element (e.g. morpho_space()/trait_space() output) raw label vector. Fixed bug group_colors() passing list without $groups element (anything intended morpho_space()/trait_space() output raw label vector) silently used list label vector instead raising documented “groups element” error.

title: intraitR 1.0.0
text: First stable release. Functionally identical 0.13.0, promoted 1.0.0 first real (non-static) validation package: maintainer ran devtools::test() actual R installation first time project’s history (every prior version validated manual code reading, independent Python reimplementation statistical logic, static analysis, lack R interpreter authoring environment). Result: 465 tests passed, 0 failures, 5 expected warnings, 6 expected skips (environment-dependent negative-path tests, e.g. “package already installed, test missing-package error” “run interactive digitizer non-interactively”). one issue surfaced, test, package: Fixed test-trait_disparity.R’s regression test exactly-2-groups permutation reshape fix (see 0.13.0 ). %% binds tighter / R’s operator precedence (?Syntax), x %% ((0:5) + 1) / 6 parsed (x %% ((0:5) + 1)) / 6 instead intended x %% (((0:5) + 1) / 6) — silently turning assertion FALSE / 6 = 0, failed expect_true() regardless whether trait_disparity() correct. Fixed parenthesising denominator explicitly. trait_disparity()’s logic fault: tests, including real statistical power check, already passed test run. changes exported function’s behaviour, arguments, return values relative 0.13.0.

title: intraitR 0.13.0
text: background_image overlay plot_landmarks() plot_fishmorph_points() (closing long-pending image-overlay task). functions gain background_image (path .jpg/.jpeg .png photograph specimen) flip_y (default TRUE) arguments: photograph drawn background layer, sized full pixel extent, digitized landmarks plotted top, visual quality control (e.g. spotting landmark placed body outline). Requires jpeg package .jpg/.jpeg files png .png files (newly Suggested, neither installed default); clear error raised relevant package missing. tools (tools::file_ext(), dispatching file extension) now declared DESCRIPTION’s Imports. meaningful original, un-aligned digitized coordinates: warning issued background_image combined "intrait_gpa" (Procrustes-aligned) object, since photograph line aligned coordinates. New internal helpers .read_background_image(), .draw_background_image(), .background_image_dims() R/utils-internal.R. Parallelization: bootstrap_functional_space(), species_sensitivity(), trait_disparity() now distribute independent resampling loops (bootstrap draws, per-individual sensitivity replacements, label permutations, respectively) across worker processes via (Suggested) future.apply package, new internal .papply() helper falls back plain vapply() future.apply installed future::plan() set — .e. purely opt-, identical results either way (reproducible across workers via future.seed = TRUE, L’Ecuyer-CMRG streams). See new “Performance large data sets” section README details, including note convex-hull complexity higher dimensions (McMullen, 1970). refactoring trait_disparity()’s permutation loop, fixed latent shape bug reshaping loop’s output t() silently corrupted p-values common exactly-two-groups case (plain vector, matrix, returned vapply()/future_vapply() FUN.VALUE length 1, t() vector produces 1 x n matrix, n x 1); replaced explicit matrix(..., byrow = TRUE) reshape handles cases correctly, new regression test. CRAN-readiness audit: added missing @return/\value documentation 17 exported print()/plot()/summary() methods lacked one (R CMD check ---cran NOTE), spanning bootstrap_functional_space(), detect_outliers(), digitization_error(), gpa_fish(), intraspecific_variability(), itv_index(), measurement_error(), morpho_space(), read_tps(), species_sensitivity(), trait_disparity(), trait_space(). Fixed plot.intrait_digitization_error(), — unlike every plot() method package — explicitly return invisible(x). Infrastructure: added pkgdown site configuration (_pkgdown.yml, thematic reference index) two new GitHub Actions workflows, pkgdown.yaml (builds deploys site gh-pages pushes default branch) test-coverage.yaml (runs covr::package_coverage() uploads results Codecov; requires CODECOV_TOKEN repository secret actually upload). Status badges added README.md.

title: intraitR 0.12.0
text: species_sensitivity() (new function). Implements “species-level sensitivity index” Bertrand (2026): species, centroid (n_axes-dimensional PCA space bootstrap_functional_space()) replaced, one individual time, individual’s position, every species held fixed centroid; replacement’s convex-hull volume expressed percent change relative unmodified centroid-based reference (fd_ref). Per species, yields mean effect (mean_dFD, mu_k Bertrand, 2026) min-max range, exposed summary table full individual-level long table. Unlike bootstrap_functional_space(), index exact deterministic (resampling, significance test): every replacement single, reproducible recomputation. print() (top species |mean_dFD|, 12 default, matching Bertrand, 2026’s figure) plot() (dot--range plot reproducing report’s Fig. 7 style, species names italicised abbreviated default) methods. Demonstrated demo(pipeline_T26_saudrune), Section 11. Requires geometry package, bootstrap_functional_space() . Internal refactor: x/groups resolution, preprocessing, PCA, n_axes selection logic shared bootstrap_functional_space() new species_sensitivity() extracted single internal helper, .fspace_pca_scores() (R/utils-internal.R), together .group_centroids(); bootstrap_functional_space()’s behaviour error messages unchanged (verified re-reading refactored code path line line, since package still executed environment – see ).

title: intraitR 0.11.1
text: Fixed devtools::document() warnings surfaced user immediately 0.11.0: several @noRd (internal, undocumented--purpose) helpers R/utils-internal.R – .plot_ordination(), .covariance_ellipse(), .kde2d(), .abbreviate_species_name() – cross-referenced using markdown link syntax (e.g. [.kde2d()]), roxygen2 can never resolve function generated .Rd page, producing permanent “resolve link topic” warning every document() run. Replaced plain code-formatted text (e.g. `.kde2d()`), always intent (pointing reader helper’s name, producing clickable cross-reference exist). one warning recur (bootstrap_functional_space .convex_hull_volume()’s doc) , per roxygen2’s message, transient first-run artifact: link target normal, exported, documented function resolves correctly starting next document() call. code behaviour changed.

title: intraitR 0.11.0
text: bootstrap_functional_space() (new function). Implements “bootstrap-based functional space estimate” Bertrand (2026, M2 internship report supervised . Toussaint S. Brosse): n_boot bootstrap “communities”, one individual drawn random per species n-dimensional convex-hull volume (functional richness) points computed (fd_boot), compared centroid-based reference volume (fd_ref, species replaced mean position). fresh PCA performed internally (many axes needed reach variance threshold, user-specified n_axes; Bertrand, 2026, used 8 axes 98% variance), convex-hull volumes computed geometry::convhulln() (new Suggested dependency, gated requireNamespace() pattern missForest trait_space(na_action = "missforest")). Reports one-sided significance test whether fd_ref sits unusually low relative bootstrap distribution. source report describes “one-sided permutation test” without fully specifying scheme; label-permutation design (reassigning species labels individuals, trait_disparity()) implemented simulation-tested rejected: collapses every permuted centroid toward global mean permuted single-individual draws keep data’s full spread, resulting null found uninformative regardless whether real intraspecific variability present, match Bertrand (2026)’s reported result. shipped implementation instead uses bootstrap distribution null (standard bootstrap percentile p-value), verified simulation correctly stay non-significant intraspecific variability negligible detect strong, real effect (see ?bootstrap_functional_space full reasoning). print() plot() methods; demonstrated demo(pipeline_T26_saudrune), Section 10. Ordination plot improvements (plot.intrait_traitspace(), plot.intrait_morphospace(), via shared internal .plot_ordination()): new legend_title, legend_italic, abbreviate_species arguments (e.g. legend_title = "Species", legend_italic = TRUE, abbreviate_species = TRUE renders "Barbatula barbatula" italic B. barbatula legend, used throughout demo(pipeline_T26_saudrune) vignette); axis ticks now short point inward (par(tcl = 0.3)); qualitative colour palette replaced higher-contrast, curated 10-colour set (falling back hcl.colors() beyond 10 groups); axis limits now computed group ellipses/hulls/density contours addition raw points, geometry never clipped plot box edge (previously possible whenever group’s dispersion ellipse extended beyond points, rule rather exception). Unused factor levels groups now dropped defensively colouring/legending.

title: intraitR 0.10.1
text: species argument added load_t26_saudrune(). "operators"/"repeatability" landmark tables keyed code (species identity lives separate "identifications" table design: landmark measurement need species, identifications can revised independently coordinates), species never column two tables absence regression. species = TRUE restores convenient access left-joining species/id_status "identifications" via code. Implemented vectorised match() lookup (new internal helper .join_species() R/utils-internal.R), merge(), specifically long-format tables many rows sharing code (one per landmark, per operator/replicate): match() preserves original row order construction, removing need verify duplicate-key join’s ordering behaviour. Modular design, matching existing operator argument’s convention: -op (warning) dataset code column, harmless -op "identifications" .

title: intraitR 0.10.0
text: Operator anonymisation (T-26 Saudrune data). operator column specimen identifiers load_t26_saudrune("operators") / load_t26_saudrune("repeatability") longer contain real names two field digitizers; replaced anonymous labels ("Operator_1", "Operator_2"), consistently across tables (matched case-insensitively original spreadsheets, person recorded different capitalisation ). Operator identity biological variable interest, nothing package’s statistical results changes. data-raw/t26_saudrune_prepare.R documents anonymisation step full provenance. Note: fixes shipped data; package’s git history ever pushed publicly earlier version files, real names may still recoverable history, outside -place data fix can address (git history rewriting separate, deliberate operation maintainer consider applies). operator argument added load_t26_saudrune() load_t26_saudrune_landmarks(), restricting returned rows/specimens one (anonymous) operators. natural way build two separate functional trait spaces, one per operator, check whether downstream results (e.g. trait_space(), fishmorph_ratios()) sensitive digitizing. Modular design: table operator column, operator ignored warning rows returned, rather raising error. style = "density" added plot.intrait_traitspace() plot.intrait_morphospace(): non-parametric kernel-density contour (highest-density-region construction, Hyndman 1996) per group, alternative parametric bivariate-normal “spider” ellipse — useful group’s points visibly skewed multimodal (can happen real digitization data). Implemented small, dependency-free bivariate Gaussian kernel density estimator (formula underlying MASS::kde2d()), new package dependency introduced. Legend placement overhauled. plot.intrait_traitspace(), plot.intrait_morphospace(), plot.intrait_itv(), plot_fishmorph_points() gained legend_position argument, defaulting "outside": group/measurement legend now drawn just outside plot box (margin) default, longer risks overlapping data points bars, whatever corner happen cluster . standard graphics::legend() position keyword (e.g. "topright") can still passed recover previous, inside--box placement.

title: intraitR 0.9.4
text: Completed 0.9.3 fix, incomplete. 0.9.3 added correct inner assignment (expect_message(x <- f(...), regexp)) three regression tests left pre-existing outer assignment place well (x <- expect_message(x <- f(...), regexp)); outer assignment executes quosure unconditionally overwrites x expect_message()’s return value, silently discarding correct one just captured inner assignment. reproduced exactly failures 0.9.2, re-running devtools::test() 0.9.3 still failed identically. outer assignment now removed test-trait_space.R, test-itv_index.R, test-trait_disparity.R, matching pattern already used correctly elsewhere test files (e.g. test-trait_space.R’s na_action = "omit" tests). trait_space(), itv_index(), trait_disparity() unchanged. , development environment R installation available verify actual devtools::test() run; please re-run locally confirm.

title: intraitR 0.9.3
text: Fixed three regression tests (test-trait_space.R, test-itv_index.R, test-trait_disparity.R, added 0.9.0 NA/unresolved-groups fix) used pattern x <- expect_message(f(...), regexp), reliably capture f()’s return value testthat (can return captured message condition instead). Corrected idiom already used elsewhere suite, expect_message(x <- f(...), regexp) (assignment inside call). test-defect: confirmed running devtools::test() real R installation first time (previous validation development environment relied static code review independent Python re-implementation, R available); underlying trait_space()/itv_index()/ trait_disparity() NA-handling logic added 0.9.0 unaffected unchanged ; three test files edited. development environment still R installation available assistant, fix (like preceding R code package) verified careful manual trace R semantics involved, re-running devtools::test() directly; please re-run full suite locally confirm relying .

title: intraitR 0.9.2
text: Added exclude_landmarks argument digitization_error(), allowing one landmark indices dropped per-landmark bias decomposition (landmark_individual, by_landmark, downstream aggregates). intended landmarks homologous biological points meaningfully comparable others landmark--landmark decomposition — notably embedded scale-bar calibration points (landmarks 20-21) FISHMORPH digitization scheme, encode fixed 1 cm real-world distance rather body landmark. demo/pipeline_T26_saudrune.R manuscript’s real-data validation (Section 4.5) now call digitization_error(..., exclude_landmarks = c(20, 21)) T-26 repeatability trial accordingly; resulting community-level bias estimate (now computed 19 anatomical landmarks ) ranking least/precise landmarks revised manuscript relative 0.9.1, scale bar’s placement incorrectly pooled genuine anatomical landmark bias.

title: intraitR 0.9.1
text: Added load_t26_saudrune_landmarks(), loads real T-26 Saudrune data (see 0.9.0, ) directly object class "intrait_landmarks", exactly format returned simulate_fishmorph_points() (coords, scale = NULL, metadata data.frame specimen, individual, species, population, replicate). makes real data set drop-replacement simulated one, runnable @examples fishmorph_segments(), fishmorph_ratios(), trait_space(), itv_index(), trait_disparity(), plot_fishmorph_points() now use instead simulate_fishmorph_points(). simulate_fishmorph_points() unchanged remains available teaching, testing, one demonstration (nested population structure itv_index()) real, single-site T-26 survey illustrate honestly.

title: intraitR 0.9.0
text: Added package’s first real (non-simulated) data set: T26_Saudrune, 279-fish landmark data set electric-fishing survey Saudrune (Adour-Garonne basin, France, 21 April 2026), covering 8 freshwater fish species (dominated Gobio occitaniae Squalius cephalus), digitized 21-landmark FISHMORPH scheme two independent operators, plus dedicated intra-operator repeatability trial (25 individuals x 9-10 replicate digitizations). Accessible via new load_t26_saudrune() function (?load_t26_saudrune); raw spreadsheets photographs distributed package, cleaned, analysis-ready tables (see data-raw/t26_saudrune_prepare.R full, transparent cleaning/QC pipeline, including every excluded specimen ). Added demo/pipeline_T26_saudrune.R (demo("pipeline_T26_saudrune")), complete worked pipeline running every stage intraitR workflow real data set: import, Generalised Procrustes Analysis, digitization quality control (including worked illustration detect_outliers() run within, across, taxonomically distinct species), FISHMORPH linear measurements ratios, functional trait space, itv_index(), measurement_error(), digitization_error(), trait_disparity(). Species identifications T26_Saudrune exposed explicit id_status field ("curated", "preliminary", "unresolved"), since small number AI-vision-assisted calls yet manually audited; per data owner’s instruction, release attempt resolve correct . Bug fix, found precisely : trait_space(), itv_index(), trait_disparity() previously mishandled groups vector containing NA (e.g. specimen unresolved identification otherwise-complete trait values, T26_Saudrune). trait_disparity() real correctness bug, just edge case: Xmat[g == lv, ] NA entry g inserts NA-valued row subset every group level (since NA == lv NA, FALSE), turning every group’s dispersion NA. itv_index()/trait_space() instead silently treated NA label size-1 pseudo-group. three functions now drop rows missing/unresolved groups value front, explicit message().

title: intraitR 0.8.0
text: Added digitization_error(), new function quantifying hierarchical digitization (operator) error repeated landmark placement, implementing protocol developed L. Boutic (2026, unpublished internship report, CRBE / INTRAIT project, supervised . Toussaint) quantify operator bias freshwater fish landmark digitization French Guiana. landmark individual, dispersion repeated digitizations around consensus position normalised reference distance (default, mean inter-landmark distance two anchor landmarks per species, exactly original protocol; standard_length_mm centroid size also available alternative, individual-level normalizations, latter addressing methodological improvement suggested original report’s discussion) aggregated hierarchically landmark individual, species, overall community bias. Includes print() plot() methods (latter reproducing report’s -landmark boxplot, ordered increasing median bias). complements existing measurement_error() (Bailey & Byrnes, 1990 / Procrustes ANOVA approach): digitization_error() deliberately GPA-free landmark--landmark, directly flag specific landmarks need stricter operational definition, whereas measurement_error() gives overall, rotation/scale-invariant repeatability estimate.

title: intraitR 0.7.5
text: Fixed stale package-level help (man/intraitR-package.Rd, ?intraitR): “Useful links” section still hard-coded old aureletoussaint/intraitR URLs, file generated roxygen2 DESCRIPTION’s URL/BugReports fields regenerated since fields updated FunTraits/intraitR 0.7.3. Fixed directly generated .Rd file; running devtools::document() now also regenerate correctly (already-correct) DESCRIPTION.

title: intraitR 0.7.4
text: Fixed real R CMD check WARNING found maintainer: demo/00Index separated demo name description two spaces, “Writing R Extensions” requires tab least three spaces, silently treated missing/empty index information. Fixed real R CMD check NOTE (“Non-standard files/directories found top level”): GITHUB_SETUP.md leftover local testing artefacts specimens.tps/P5180033.jpg now excluded built package via .Rbuildignore (already excluded git via .gitignore, affect R CMD build/check). remaining NOTE (“checking future file timestamps … unable verify current time”) package issue: means checking machine reach time-verification service network, unrelated file package.

title: intraitR 0.7.3
text: Repository moved https://github.com/FunTraits/intraitR; URL, BugReports (DESCRIPTION), inst/CITATION, remotes::install_github() example README.md updated accordingly. Added standard GitHub Actions R CMD check workflow (.github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml, Linux release/devel/oldrel, macOS, Windows). .gitignore extended exclude .DS_Store two local testing artefacts part package source.

title: intraitR 0.7.2
text: Hand-computed-precision tests compared unrounded expected values itv_index()’s default digits = 4-rounded percentages tolerance tight rounding (rounding small percentage shifts relative difference far large one) — fixed passing digits = 12 tests. (2) FISHMORPH test called nlevels() directly fish$metadata$species, plain character vector rather factor, always returns 0 — fixed wrapping factor() first. itv_index()’s code unaffected either issue.

title: intraitR 0.7.1
text: Bug fix: itv_index(nested = ...) incorrectly errored (“level nested must belong single level groups”) whenever population labels reused identically across species — exactly simulate_fishmorph_points()/simulate_fish_landmarks() (population "Pop_1"/"Pop_2" every species), common real data . Found maintainer running itv_nested vignette/help example verbatim. Fixed: nested levels longer need globally unique; combination groups nested now automatically treated distinct population (via interaction(groups, nested)), exactly nesting operator aov(y ~ Error(groups/nested)) handle . strict “must belong single group” validation error removed longer necessary.

title: intraitR 0.7.0
text: New function itv_index(): decomposes total trait variance interspecific (-group, e.g. -species) component intraspecific trait variability (ITV) component, following variance-partitioning approach Violle et al. (2012) de Bello et al. (2011) (%ITV = 100 x SS_within / SS_total). Accepts optional nested grouping factor (e.g. population within species) split ITV component -population within-population (residual) parts, following within-/among-population distinction used ITV meta-analyses (Siefert et al., 2015); nested decomposition exact design, including unbalanced group population sizes. Returns per-trait breakdown multivariate summary aggregated across (optionally standardised) traits, dedicated print stacked-bar-chart plot methods.

title: intraitR 0.6.1
text: New demo, demo("na_handling", package = "intraitR"): simulates FISHMORPH data set, deletes known set trait values, compares "impute_mean", "impute_group_mean", "missforest" RMSE true (deleted) values, alongside "omit" default "fail" behaviour trait_space().

title: intraitR 0.6.0
text: trait_space() gains na_action = "missforest": nonparametric random-forest imputation missing trait values via missForest::missForest() (Stekhoven & Bühlmann, 2012), using groups (supplied) auxiliary predictor. Unlike "impute_mean"/ "impute_group_mean", exploits correlations among traits generally preferable values missing. Reports number values imputed --bag normalised RMSE via message(). Requires (new, Suggests-) missForest package; results stochastic unless set.seed() called beforehand. New arguments missforest_ntree (default 100) missforest_maxiter (default 10) control underlying random forests.

title: intraitR 0.5.1
text: Bug fix: digitize_landmarks() called geomorph::digitize2d() incorrect argument name (image.list, exist geomorph) instead actual argument name, filelist, causing immediate “unused argument” error every call. Found maintainer testing real photograph. Fixed using filelist, ... documentation corrected non-existent MultCurvatures argument real scale/MultScale/verbose arguments geomorph::digitize2d().

title: intraitR 0.5.0
text: New function digitize_landmarks(): convenience wrapper around geomorph::digitize2d() point--click digitization landmarks directly specimen photographs, following either fixed 21/22-point FISHMORPH scheme (scheme = "fishmorph") user-specified number generic landmarks (scheme = "generic"). Digitized coordinates written tpsDig file immediately re-read read_tps(), result ready--use "intrait_landmarks" object. Requires interactive graphics device; stops informative error rather hanging called non-interactively (e.g. scripts, knitted vignettes, automated tests).

title: intraitR 0.4.0
text: New function trait_disparity(): tests whether groups (e.g. species) differ multivariate dispersion functional traits, using permutation test trait variance (trace group’s trait covariance matrix), computed full standardised trait matrix built trait_space() (.e. truncated two plotting axes). complements intraspecific_variability(), reports shape disparity univariate coefficients variation test group differences multivariate trait dispersion. Accepts either "intrait_traitspace" object raw trait table groups. trait_space() now returns additional (previously internal) element X, standardised trait matrix actually analysed (post log-transformation removal constant columns, centred/scaled requested), used trait_disparity(). additive change affect existing use trait_space()’s output. trait_space() gains na_action argument ("fail" (default, unchanged behaviour), "omit", "impute_mean", "impute_group_mean") handling missing values numeric trait columns, instead always erroring. "omit" imputation modes report, via message(), many rows dropped values imputed, operation never silent. New function detect_outliers(): quality-control screen landmark digitization errors, flagging specimens whose Procrustes distance sample consensus shape exceeds robust (median + threshold x MAD) cut-, spirit geomorph::plotOutliers(). Includes diagnostic plot ranked table atypical specimens.

title: intraitR 0.3.0
text: trait_space() gains log_transform argument (default TRUE): numeric traits log10(x + 1)-transformed centring/scaling ordination, standard practice ratio-type functional traits bounded zero often right-skewed (+ 1 accommodates traits legitimately equal 0 Villéger et al., 2010, exception rules implemented fishmorph_ratios()). Set log_transform = FALSE disable. apply , used , morpho_space(), ordinates Procrustes shape coordinates rather trait ratios. plot.intrait_morphospace() plot.intrait_traitspace() gain style argument ("spider", "hull", "none"), replacing previous convex_hull logical. new default, style = "spider", displays group individual points, dashed segments linking every point group mean, group mean , ellipse_level (default 95%) dispersion ellipse bivariate-normal approximation — classical “spider”/“star” plot used depict group structure ordination diagrams. style = "hull" reproduces previous convex-hull display.

title: intraitR 0.2.2
text: trait_space()’s “fewer two numeric columns” error message now reads “… least two numeric columns removing constant (zero-variance) columns.” (kept original wording intact downstream code/tests matching “least two numeric columns” still work) instead reworded message accidentally broke match. Test suite: warnings expected, documented side effect trait_space() (dropping non-numeric/constant columns) now suppressed suppressWarnings() tests don’t specifically test , dedicated test asserts warnings raised expected.

title: intraitR 0.2.1
text: Bug fixes found devtools::test() real R installation: trait_space() now drops constant (zero-variance) numeric columns automatically, warning, instead erroring inside stats::prcomp()/stats::cmdscale() (“rescale constant/zero column unit variance”). commonly occurred incidental numeric metadata carried fishmorph_segments() / fishmorph_ratios() (e.g. digitization replicate counter constant n_replicates = 1) passed trait_space() unfiltered. summary_traits() trait_space() now check “usable numeric columns” warning dropped non-numeric columns, instead emitting spurious warning right erroring. Fixed unit tests test-fishmorph_ratios.R compared rounded output (default digits = 4) un-rounded expected values ratios repeating decimals (REs, RMl).

title: intraitR 0.2.0
text: landmarks per specimen (snout, caudal fin basis, body depth, head depth, eye position/diameter, mouth, pectoral fin, caudal peduncle caudal fin depth, plus embedded scale bar optional body-curvature correction point). fishmorph_segments() computes 11 linear measurements protocol (Bl, Bd, Hd, Eh, Mo, PFi, PFl, Ed, Jl, CPd, CFd) directly digitized points, automatically converting pixel units centimetres using embedded scale bar, applying optional body-curvature correction standard length. fishmorph_ratios() computes 9 unitless FISHMORPH ratios (BEl, VEp, REs, OGp, RMl, BLs, PFv, PFs, CPt) measurements, optionally adds maximum body length (MBl), implements special-case rules Villeger et al. (2010) species without visible caudal fin, ventrally positioned mouth, without pectoral fins. trait_space() builds generic functional trait space (PCA PCoA) numeric trait table, group convex-hull plotting; morpho_space() now shares plotting code trait_space(). plot_fishmorph_points() visualises 21/22-point digitization scheme specimen, following colour scheme original protocol figure. simulate_fishmorph_points() generates simulated multi-species landmark data following FISHMORPH point scheme, examples, teaching testing functions .

title: intraitR 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Landmark import TPS files (read_tps()) generic long-format CSV files (read_landmarks_csv()). Generalised Procrustes Analysis wrapper (gpa_fish()) built geomorph::gpagen(). Linear inter-landmark distances (linear_distances()) classical fish morphometric ratios (morpho_ratios()). Morphological space construction plotting (morpho_space()). Allometry correction (correct_allometry()). Intraspecific morphological variability, combining shape disparity (geomorph::morphol.disparity()) coefficients variation linear traits (intraspecific_variability()). Measurement error / repeatability analysis replicated digitization, univariate traits (ANOVA-based percent measurement error repeatability, Bailey & Byrnes 1990) shape data (Procrustes ANOVA, Fruciano 2016) via measurement_error(). Landmark configuration plotting (plot_landmarks()) trait summary tables (summary_traits()). Simulated example data set fish_landmarks generator function simulate_fish_landmarks().
